fields.text:
# Surface task landings in the read CLI

## Goal

Make the git-landing data that `endless worktree land` already records visible
in the task read surface. Every land emits a `task.landed` event; the Go
projector writes one row per land into the `task_landings` table
(`task_id`, `session_id`, `branch`, `merge_commit_sha`, `landed_at`). Landing
is append-only (a task can land more than once) and does NOT change task status.

## Decisions

1. **`Landed:` line in `task show`** — most recent `landed_at` (formatted by
   `_format_timestamp`, like the existing `Confirmed:` line) + the latest
   land's short merge SHA, with a `(landed N times)` suffix only when more than
   one landing row exists. Shown only when at least one landing row exists.
   Example: `Landed:     2026-06-14 9:31 pm  160272f  (landed 5 times)`
   Added to all three outputs: default human, `--llm`, and `--json`.

2. **`endless task landed` command (arg-driven)**:
   - Bare `task landed` → lists tasks that have at least one landing, ordered by
     most-recent `landed_at` descending (peer to `task active` / `task recent`).
   - `task landed <id>` → that task's full landing history: every row
     (`landed_at`, `branch`, `merge_commit_sha`), newest first.

3. **Read path — Python now, migrate to Go later (decided with Mike
   2026-06-17).** The original plan gated this work behind E-894 (move
   task-display reads to Python→Go) so the landing reads would land directly on
   the Go path. Mike unblocked it: seeing what has and hasn't landed without
   writing SQL is a live pain point, and the data is already recorded (75
   landings at decision time). The reads ship now on the existing Python
   `db.query` path. When E-894 / E-1482 (`query_bridge.py`) move the
   task-display reads to Go, the landing reads (`_task_landings`, `landed_list`,
   `landed_item`) belong in that same migration.

## Implementation (2026-06-17, this task)

In `src/endless/task_cmd.py`:
- `_task_landings(item_id)` — all landing rows for a task, newest first.
- `_format_landed_line(landings)` — renders `TS  shortsha  (landed N times)`,
  count suffix only when >1, tolerates a missing SHA.
- `detail_item()` fetches landings once and emits the `Landed:` line / `landed=`
  field / `"landed"` JSON object across the three render paths.
- `landed_list()` + `_render_landed_table()` — bare-list command body
  (ID / Landed / Lands / Title), grouped by project, with `--llm` / `--json`.
- `landed_item()` — per-task landing history, with `--llm` / `--json`.

In `src/endless/cli.py`:
- `task landed` command with an optional `item_id` argument plus
  `--project / --all / --limit / --llm / --json`.

Tests: `tests/test_task_landed.py` (14 cases) — `_format_landed_line` units,
the `Landed:` line across human/llm/json, never-landed omission, and
`task landed` list + history including ordering and the empty case.

## Verification

- `endless task show <landed-id>` shows the `Landed:` line with timestamp +
  short SHA; a 5-landing task renders `(landed 5 times)`; a single-landing task
  has no suffix; a never-landed task shows no `Landed:` line.
- `endless task landed` lists landed tasks newest-first;
  `endless task landed <id>` shows full history newest-first; never-landed shows
  "Never landed".
- `--llm` and `--json` carry the landing fields in both commands.
- `uv run pytest tests/test_task_landed.py` → 14 passed.

payload.title: Stop handoff render from materializing untracked templates into a self_dev project's main

fields.text:
# E-1589 — Make handoff template materialization self_dev-aware

## Symptom

`just land` refuses with "main has uncommitted user changes" naming
`.endless/templates/`. Hit on three consecutive task landings. The files are
untracked copies of the handoff templates that appear in the **main checkout**.

## Root cause

`render_spawn_handoff` (`src/endless/task_cmd.py:3194`) shells out to
`endless-go template render handoff/<type>` **without `--project` and without
setting `cwd`**. So `resolveProjectRoot("")` (`internal/templatecmd/template.go:107`)
falls to `projectRootFromCwd()`, which walks up from the **invoking process's
cwd** to the nearest `.endless`. When `task spawn` / `task show --handoff` runs
from the main checkout (the normal case), that resolves to **main**, and
`materializeIfMissing` (`template.go:80`/`188`) writes the embedded template into
`<main>/.endless/templates/handoff/<type>.md.tmpl` as a side effect of a render
(a read). Those files are neither gitignored nor committed → untracked → block
`just land`.

Surfaced now because E-1566 split the single handoff template into four per-type
variants (`task/bug/research/epic`); none had been materialized, so the first
render of each type from main drops a new untracked file.

## Why not the obvious fixes

- **Commit / auto-commit the files in endless's own repo** re-creates a quieter
  bug: lookup order is `.local.tmpl` → `.tmpl` (committed) → embedded, so a
  committed on-disk copy **shadows the embedded source** that endless development
  edits. `materializeIfMissing` never overwrites, so future edits to
  `internal/templatecmd/templates/` would have no effect — the renderer keeps
  using the stale committed copy.
- **Gitignore `.endless/templates/`** has the same shadow problem (present-but-
  ignored still shadows embedded) and contradicts the "committed copy" design for
  consumer projects.

## Fix: gate materialization on `self_dev`

`self_dev` is exactly the flag that means "this repo *is* endless" vs "this repo
*uses* endless." Endless's own templates are the embedded source; consumer
templates live on disk.

| Project | render behavior |
|---|---|
| `self_dev: true` (endless itself) | **Skip materialize — render from embedded.** No file written to main, no shadow, land never blocks. |
| `self_dev: false` (consumer projects) | **Write-on-read + auto-commit** the materialized file. Zero-thought UX: the on-disk copy is the customizable template, committed so it is tracked/discoverable. |

The write-on-read convenience is preserved for end users (the decided, desirable
behavior); the burden of editing embedded templates correctly falls on the
endless developer, which is acceptable.

## Implementation

In `internal/templatecmd/template.go`:

1. Export a self_dev check from `internal/monitor` (thin wrapper over the
   existing unexported `projectIsSelfDev` at `internal/monitor/db.go:208`), e.g.
   `monitor.ProjectIsSelfDev(root string) bool`.
2. In `runRender`, after `resolveProjectRoot`:
   - if `monitor.ProjectIsSelfDev(projectRoot)` → **skip** `materializeIfMissing`
     (rendering still works: `loadTemplate` already falls back to embedded).
   - else → `materializeIfMissing`, then **auto-commit** the written file.
3. Auto-commit (consumer projects only), keep it narrow and safe:
   - commit **only the materialized file's pathspec** (`git -C <projectRoot> add
     <relpath>` then `git commit -m "<msg>" -- <relpath>`), never a blanket
     `git add -A`, so unrelated working-tree changes are untouched.
   - guard: only when `<projectRoot>` is a git work tree; on any git failure,
     log and continue (a failed auto-commit must NOT fail the render).
   - message e.g. `Endless: materialize handoff template <name>`.

No internal `E-NNNN` IDs in any user-facing string.

## Verification

- **self_dev (endless):** from the main checkout, `endless task show <id>
  --handoff` (or a spawn) produces output and creates **no** `.endless/templates/`
  files; deleting any embedded edit shows the change takes effect (no shadow).
- **consumer (self_dev unset) fixture:** first render writes
  `.endless/templates/handoff/<type>.md.tmpl` AND commits exactly that file
  (clean `git status` afterward, one new commit touching only that path); second
  render is a no-op (file exists). In a non-git dir, render still succeeds and
  leaves the file untracked without erroring.
- Existing `internal/templatecmd` tests still pass; add cases for the self_dev
  skip and the consumer auto-commit.

## Cleanup

Remove the stray `.endless/templates/` left in main by prior occurrences and by
the spawn of this task itself (`rm -rf .endless/templates` from the main
checkout) before landing.

payload.title: Add a way to live-test consumer template materialization from a self-dev sandbox
payload.description: E-1589 gates template materialization on self_dev, so endless's own sandbox always takes the skip branch and the materialize+auto-commit (consumer) path can't be exercised by running endless commands in-sandbox. Today it's covered only by Go tests and by targeting a registered non-self_dev project via 'template render --project <name>'. Consider a first-class way to live-test the consumer path from the sandbox (e.g. a flag forcing consumer behavior, or a throwaway non-self_dev fixture project). Maybe — only if live-testing this path proves valuable.

payload.title: Fix session shell helpers (esp/esf) failing in self-dev worktrees by passing --db main
payload.description: _endless_run (src/endless/cli.py _SHELL_INIT_SNIPPET) routes session-helper calls through the active session's worktree with 'uv run --directory <wt> endless ...'. When that worktree belongs to a self_dev project (endless itself), the mandatory --db gate (WORKTREE_DB_REFUSAL) fires and esp/esf fail because no --db value is supplied. These helpers only ever wrap 'session use/cd/forget', which operate on the real session ledger, never a per-worktree sandbox — so the wrapper should always target --db main. Likely fix: have _endless_run pass '--db main' on the 'uv run --directory' path (covers esu/esp/esf, since all session commands target the real DB). Distinct from E-1306 (esp auto-resolve guard) and E-1254 (claim-output wording), but touches the same shell snippet — coordinate edits.

fields.title: Gate just install global side effects to main and add worktree-scoped install

fields.text:
# E-1036 — Gate `just install` global side effects to main; worktree-scoped install

## Context

`just install` has two globally-observable side effects:
- `ln -sfn "$(pwd)/bin/endless-go" /usr/local/bin/endless-go`
- `uv tool install -e . --force`

Run from a **git worktree**, these repoint the *system-default* `endless-go`
symlink and Python tool at the worktree's transient binary/source — silently
serving stale code to every `--db main` invocation and all non-worktree endless
use across all projects until someone notices.

**Live incident (2026-06-15):** a `just install` run from worktree `e-1565` left
`/usr/local/bin/endless-go` pointing at e-1565's binary for ~2 days. E-1585's
landed `config.json` seeding therefore did not run during `endless task spawn`
(spawn uses the global binary under `--db main`), producing a sandbox with no
`config.json`. Surfaced while verifying E-1585.

E-1036's original "refuse" implementation was lost: a tmux crash killed the
session that did the work, and an older worktree reaper (age-based, no
merge-status check) removed its worktree before the work was confirmed merged —
hence this ticket's stale `verify` and the dead, pre-E-1367
`task/1036-just-install-worktree-guard` branch (ignore it; it would revert the
binary consolidation).

## Decision (decided — implement as written)

Don't merely refuse from a worktree; **overload `just install` by checkout
context** and add an explicit worktree-scoped form. The invariant:
**worktree-scoping is achieved via injection (`.claude/settings.json` + PATH/XDG),
NEVER by mutating the global symlink or the global `uv` tool.**

- `just install` from the **main checkout** → today's global install
  (symlink + `uv tool install -e .`). Unchanged.
- `just install` from a **worktree** → do the worktree-**scoped** setup instead
  of the global side effects, and print that it scoped to the worktree and left
  global untouched. (Turns the exact footgun reflex into the right outcome.)
- `just install-worktree` → explicit scoped bundle, callable anywhere it makes
  sense; never runs `ln -sfn` or `uv tool install`.

Worktree-scoping is what the existing recipes already do safely (E-998 / E-1281):
`go-work-init` + `build` + `claude-settings-init` + `dev-sandbox-init`. So the
scoped path is a bundle of recipes that already exist and are already injection-
based.

## Implementation (justfile)

1. Detect checkout context with the git-dir vs git-common-dir discriminator
   already used in `claude-settings-init` (justfile ~226-231) and
   `dev-sandbox-init`: equal → main; differ → worktree.
2. Refactor `install` to branch on it:
   - **main:** existing global steps (`build`, symlink, `uv tool install -e .`).
   - **worktree:** delegate to `install-worktree`; do NOT run the global steps;
     `echo` what was wired and that `/usr/local/bin` was left untouched.
3. Add recipe `install-worktree`: runs `go-work-init` + `build` +
   `claude-settings-init` + `dev-sandbox-init` (the existing scoped recipes).
   This is the explicit form; `just install` from a worktree calls it. (A
   sibling recipe is used rather than a `--worktree` flag because `just`'s flag
   passing is awkward; `just install-worktree` is the canonical explicit
   spelling. Functionally this is the "`just install --worktree`" Mike asked
   for.)
4. Hardening (second layer, keep minimal): in `install` (both paths), warn
   loudly if `/usr/local/bin/endless-go` resolves into a `.endless/worktrees/`
   path — surfaces an already-mispointed symlink instead of letting it fester.

## Verification

- **From main:** `just install` → `/usr/local/bin/endless-go` → main's
  `bin/endless-go`; `endless-go` runs main code. (Global path unchanged.)
- **From a worktree:** `just install` → `/usr/local/bin/endless-go` still points
  at **main** (confirm `ls -la`); the worktree's `.claude/settings.json` +
  `bin-sandbox/` are wired; output states "scoped to worktree, global untouched".
- `just install-worktree` from a worktree → same scoped result.
- The stale-symlink warning fires when the symlink points into a worktree.

## Coordination

- `relates_to` E-986 (pluggable post-worktree-create hook) and E-1039
  (worktree-local binary preference) — adjacent worktree-toolchain work.
- Supersedes the original "refuse" mechanism (evolved: refuse → scoped install).

fields.description: just install's global side effects (ln -sfn into /usr/local/bin, uv tool install -e .) silently hijack the system-default endless toolchain for every session/project when run from a worktree (live incident 2026-06-15: stale e-1565 binary served for ~2 days). Overload just install by checkout context: from main it does the global install; from a worktree it does the worktree-SCOPED setup (settings.json + PATH/XDG injection) and never touches global. Add an explicit install-worktree recipe. Original refuse-based impl was lost to a tmux crash + age-based worktree reap (stale verify). See plan.

payload.title: Add 'endless setup shell-helpers' subcommand to install the esu/esp/esf eval line into ~/.zshrc

fields.text:
# E-1592 — `endless setup shell-helpers` subcommand

## Context

`endless setup` (`src/endless/setup.py`, wired in `cli.py` ~2049) installs three
integrations as install/remove subcommand pairs — `prompt-hook`, `claude-hook`,
`channel-plugin`. **None install the `esu`/`esp`/`esf` session shell helpers.**
Users install those by hand, and the two docs that explain how disagree: the
`shell-init` command docstring (`cli.py:677`) says static append
(`endless shell-init >> ~/.zshrc`), while the agent guide
(`docs/guide/orchestration.md:126`, `index.md:29`) says `eval "$(endless shell-init)"`.

Goal: a first-class installer, consistent with the existing setup pattern, that
adds the helpers and keeps them current.

## Decisions (made with Mike)

1. **New subcommand pair `setup shell-helpers` / `setup remove-shell-helpers`** —
   parallel to the existing three integrations. NOT folded into `prompt-hook`
   (that command is activity-monitoring; session navigation is a separate
   concern). Two commands for a full setup is acceptable.
2. **Install the `eval` line, not the static snippet.** `endless shell-init` is
   pure static stdout (~30–50ms, no DB I/O), so regenerating it once per shell
   launch is negligible — and it means snippet fixes (e.g. the E-1591 `--db main`
   fix) reach users on next shell with no rc surgery. This removes the
   "find the marker block and replace it manually" friction the docstring laments.
3. **Fail loudly — no PATH guard.** Write a bare `eval "$(endless shell-init)"`
   (NOT `command -v endless && ...`). If `endless` isn't on PATH, the user should
   see the shell-startup error, not silently lose the helpers. Mike's call.

## Change

**`src/endless/setup.py`**
- Add `install_shell_helpers()` / `remove_shell_helpers()`, modeled on
  `setup_prompt_hook` / `remove_prompt_hook` (same `~/.zshrc` target prompt,
  same idempotency check via a marker, same confirm + manual-fallback UX).
- Block to append (marker comment lets remove find it):
  ```sh
  # Endless: session shell helpers (esu/esp/esf)
  eval "$(endless shell-init)"
  ```
- Idempotency: detect an existing install by the marker comment or the
  `eval "$(endless shell-init)"` line.

**`src/endless/cli.py`**
- Register `@setup.command("shell-helpers")` and
  `@setup.command("remove-shell-helpers")`, mirroring the existing pairs.
- Update the `shell_init` docstring (`cli.py:677`) to stop recommending the
  stale static `>>` form and point at `endless setup shell-helpers` (and the
  guarded-free `eval` line for manual installs), reconciling it with the guide.

**Tests** — add a `tests/test_setup_shell_helpers.py` (or extend existing setup
coverage if added later): asserts the block is appended, idempotency (no double
append), and removal. Follow `test_shell_init.py` style.

## Out of scope
- bash support beyond `~/.zshrc` (existing setup is zsh-only; keep parity).
- A combined top-level `endless setup all` runner.

## Verification
1. `endless setup shell-helpers` against a scratch rcfile → appends the marker
   block once; re-running reports already-installed (no duplicate).
2. `endless setup remove-shell-helpers` → removes only the marker block.
3. New shell sourcing the rcfile has `esu`/`esp`/`esf` defined
   (`declare -f esu`); with `endless` removed from PATH the eval errors loudly.
4. `just test` green.

payload.title: Add 'task respawn' command to spawn into prior session
Fix stale TestShouldSkipForWorktreeAt tests asserting on removed bin/endless-hook path
payload.description: TestShouldSkipForWorktreeAt_WorktreeBinaryMissing and _SelfIsGlobal in internal/hookcmd/claude_skip_test.go fail on clean main. They assert WARN/skip behavior keyed on a <worktree>/bin/endless-hook binary, but E-1367 consolidated the per-binary hooks into a single endless-go binary, so that path no longer exists. Update the tests to the endless-go reality, or retire them if the skip behavior they pinned is now obsolete.

fields.text:
# E-1571 — Add sessions schema for bg-agent rows (active_epic_id, kind, short_id, nullable session_id)

Lands after E-1565 / E-1566 (per-type templates, not directly dependent) and **before** E-1568 (--bg dispatch writes these columns) and E-1570 (attach verbs may read kind to discriminate fg vs bg).

This child consolidates all bg-agent schema changes in one place: the new columns AND the nullable-session_id rebuild needed because bg rows have no UUID at dispatch time.

## Decisions

### Schema additions
Three new columns on `sessions`, plus one constraint relaxation:

- `active_epic_id INTEGER` — nullable FK to `tasks(id)`. Semantics:
  - **Coordinator (epic) fg row:** stable = the epic's task id; `active_task_id` mutates as user switches view to children.
  - **Bg-agent row:** `active_task_id` = the child task id; `active_epic_id` = parent epic id (resolved at spawn time by Python).
  - **Non-epic fg row:** NULL.
- `kind TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'tmux'` — discriminator: `'tmux'` or `'background'`. App-level validation (no CHECK, per house rule). Backfill: all existing rows become `'tmux'`.
- `short_id TEXT` — nullable, generic across harnesses (Claude's ~8-hex handle today; future Codex/OpenCode equivalents reuse the same column). The harness discriminator is the existing `sessions.platform` column; format is harness-determined. UNIQUE constraint via partial index (only enforced where NOT NULL).
- **`session_id` constraint relaxed**: drop `NOT NULL`. Bg rows are inserted with `session_id = NULL` at dispatch time and UPDATEd to the real UUID when the bg agent's SessionStart hook fires (the UUID isn't knowable until then). UNIQUE constraint preserved (SQLite treats NULL as distinct in unique indexes, so multiple pending bg rows coexist safely).

`process` column (the tmux pane id) is already nullable. Semantics shift: `kind='background'` rows legitimately leave it NULL. Document the invariant in a comment.

### SQLite table-rebuild for nullable session_id
SQLite has no `ALTER COLUMN DROP NOT NULL`; the documented path is a full table rebuild. The change file performs this in a single transaction with `PRAGMA foreign_keys = OFF`:

```sql
PRAGMA foreign_keys = OFF;
BEGIN;

CREATE TABLE sessions_new (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    session_id TEXT,                         -- was NOT NULL
    project_id INTEGER,
    platform TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'claude',
    state TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'working',
    active_task_id INTEGER,
    plan_file_path TEXT,
    process TEXT,
    started_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S', 'now')),
    last_activity TEXT,
    transcript_offset INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    transcript_path TEXT,
    summary TEXT,
    hidden INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    needs_recap INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    summary_seq INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    active_epic_id INTEGER,                  -- new
    kind TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'tmux',       -- new
    short_id TEXT,                           -- new
    UNIQUE (session_id),
    FOREIGN KEY (project_id) REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    FOREIGN KEY (active_task_id) REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    FOREIGN KEY (active_epic_id) REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE SET NULL
);

INSERT INTO sessions_new (id, session_id, project_id, platform, state, active_task_id,
    plan_file_path, process, started_at, last_activity, transcript_offset,
    transcript_path, summary, hidden, needs_recap, summary_seq)
SELECT id, session_id, project_id, platform, state, active_task_id,
    plan_file_path, process, started_at, last_activity, transcript_offset,
    transcript_path, summary, hidden, needs_recap, summary_seq
FROM sessions;

DROP TABLE sessions;
ALTER TABLE sessions_new RENAME TO sessions;

-- Re-create the two end-of-life triggers (dropped with the old table).
CREATE TRIGGER sessions_null_process_on_end_update
AFTER UPDATE OF state ON sessions
WHEN NEW.state = 'ended' AND NEW.process IS NOT NULL
BEGIN
    UPDATE sessions SET process = NULL WHERE id = NEW.id;
END;

CREATE TRIGGER sessions_null_process_on_end_insert
AFTER INSERT ON sessions
WHEN NEW.state = 'ended' AND NEW.process IS NOT NULL
BEGIN
    UPDATE sessions SET process = NULL WHERE id = NEW.id;
END;

-- short_id uniqueness via partial index (only when NOT NULL).
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_sessions_short_id_unique
    ON sessions(short_id) WHERE short_id IS NOT NULL;

COMMIT;
PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;
```

FKs from other tables (`session_messages.session_id REFERENCES sessions(session_id)`, `session_gates.session_id REFERENCES sessions(session_id)`) reference by name and resolve correctly after the RENAME. The `PRAGMA foreign_keys = OFF` block prevents transient violations during the swap.

### Window-name / status-line rendering
Status-line `[E-NNN]` prefix in `internal/tmuxcmd/status_line.go::format()` (line 69) becomes:

- `active_epic_id IS NULL` → `[E-NNNN]` (today's behavior, unchanged)
- `active_epic_id IS NOT NULL AND active_epic_id = active_task_id` → `[E-EEEE]` (viewing the epic itself)
- `active_epic_id IS NOT NULL AND active_epic_id != active_task_id` → `[E-EEEE:E-CCCC]` (viewing a child of the epic)

Same logic applied to `internal/tmuxcmd/show_menu.go::format()` (line 75) for the popup header.

`monitor.ActiveTaskInfo` (the struct the format functions read) gains `ActiveEpicID *int64`. The query that populates it joins `sessions` (it already does) and selects `active_epic_id`.

### Coordinator row vs child-view: how active_task_id moves
Out of scope here — this child only adds the columns. The mechanism for "user switches focus to a child" is part of E-1570 (attach verbs) and the day-to-day `endless task claim` flow. This child does NOT implement focus-switching; it only ensures the data shape exists.

### "Focused bg agent" derivation
Per E-1552 outcome ("derived from the coordinator row, not stored"), no column added. Queries that need it compute it: `SELECT * FROM sessions WHERE kind='background' AND active_task_id = <coordinator's active_task_id>` — i.e. the bg agent whose child task matches what the coordinator is viewing. This child adds a Go helper `monitor.FocusedBgAgent(coordinatorSessionID) (*Session, error)` so callers don't reinvent the SQL.

### Validation
Go-side validator (`internal/monitor/sessions.go` or wherever existing validation lives — locate via grep at implementation): reject any insert/update where `kind NOT IN ('tmux','background')`. Mirrors the TaskType pattern (E-1538). No SQL CHECK.

Startup integrity check warns (not fails) if a `kind='background'` row has `process IS NOT NULL` or if a `kind='tmux'` row has `process IS NULL` while `state='working'` — the schema doesn't enforce these, but drift is interesting.

## Files

### New
- `internal/schema/changes/e-1571-sessions-bg-agent-shape.sql` — full table-rebuild block above.

### Changed
- `internal/schema/schema.sql` — declare the post-migration shape: `session_id TEXT` (no NOT NULL) with `UNIQUE`; add `active_epic_id`, `kind`, `short_id` columns; add the partial unique index on `short_id`. Comment block above the new columns explaining the kind discriminator, active_epic_id semantics, and short_id's harness-agnostic role.
- `internal/monitor/sessions.go` (or whichever file holds session CRUD — locate at impl): expose `ActiveEpicID *int64`, `Kind string`, `ShortID *string` on the Go session struct; include in SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE queries; validate `kind` on write; allow NULL `session_id` on `kind='background'` inserts.
- `internal/monitor/tmux.go` — extend `ActiveTaskInfo` with `ActiveEpicID *int64`, populate from query.
- `internal/tmuxcmd/status_line.go::format()` — branch on `info.ActiveEpicID`.
- `internal/tmuxcmd/show_menu.go::format()` — same branch.
- `internal/monitor/sessions_focus.go` (new file is fine, or fold into existing) — `FocusedBgAgent(coordinatorSessionID)` helper.

### Tests
- `internal/monitor/sessions_test.go`:
  - Round-trip a row with `kind='background'` + `active_epic_id` + `short_id` set + `session_id IS NULL`.

# E-1568 — Add --bg flag to endless task spawn that dispatches via claude --bg --name carrier

Lands after **E-1565** (renderer), **E-1566** (type variants), **E-1571** (sessions schema: short_id, kind, active_epic_id, nullable session_id), and **E-1569** (bgIsolation:'none' in settings). This is the centerpiece of the epic.

## Decisions

### CLI surface
- New flag `--bg` on `endless task spawn <id>`.
- `--worktree <path>` works in both fg and bg paths: in bg, it sets `subprocess.run(..., cwd=<path>)` so the bg agent inherits a non-default cwd. Default remains the spawn-created worktree at `.endless/worktrees/e-<id>/`. (Not mutually exclusive — the only operational caveat is that a bg agent's filesystem context is invisible until you `attach`; if you set `--worktree` and never attach, the override is silent. Harmless.)
- Compatible with `--reopen`, `--force`, `--no-plan` (the last is a no-op — bg agents don't have a `/plan` slash-key concept; document and ignore).
- Compatible with `--type` of the target task (renderer picks the right handoff variant).

### Invocation
Per `docs/research-2026-06-12-claude-background-agents.md` §2/§7:
```bash
claude --bg --name "E-<task_id>" "<rendered-handoff-text>"
```
Prompt is positional argv (handoff is ~2 KB — well under Darwin's ARG_MAX of 256 KB). Stdout returns:
```
backgrounded · <8hex> · E-<task_id>
  claude attach <8hex>    open in this terminal
  ...
```

### Short-ID capture
Parse the first stdout line matching `^backgrounded · ([a-f0-9]+) · `. Regex pinned in code with comment + research-doc citation. On no-match, raise `ClickException` with the raw stdout — never silently proceed with a missing id.

### Session row write
At pre-claim time (BEFORE invoking claude), spawn already creates the worktree and flips status to `in_progress`. After the `claude --bg` subprocess returns and short-id is parsed, INSERT into `sessions`:

| Column | Value |
|---|---|
| `session_id` | **NULL** — bg agents have no UUID at dispatch. The bg agent's `SessionStart` hook UPDATEs this column with the real UUID once it fires (see Hook update below). SQLite's UNIQUE constraint treats NULL as distinct, so multiple pending bg rows coexist. (`session_id NOT NULL` relaxed in E-1571.) |
| `project_id` | resolved from task row |
| `platform` | `'claude'` |
| `state` | `'working'` |
| `active_task_id` | `<task_id>` |
| `active_epic_id` | parent epic id resolved by `WITH RECURSIVE` walk up `tasks.parent_id` to the nearest type=epic ancestor (NULL if none) |
| `kind` | `'background'` |
| `process` | NULL (bg agents have no tmux pane) |
| `short_id` | `<short_id>` from the dispatch stdout. (Generic column added in E-1571; harness-agnostic, format determined by `platform`.) |
| `started_at` | now |

This row IS the binding from the endless side. `short_id` is the durable handle: `claude attach <short_id>` works, the row survives the UUID arriving later, queries can locate the bg row by either `short_id` or (eventually) `session_id`.

### SessionStart hook update (bg-context decoration)
The bg agent's `SessionStart` hook fires inside the bg session with `source=startup` and the real `payload.SessionID` (UUID). Hook handler (`internal/hookcmd/claude.go::handleSessionStart`) gets a new branch:

```
if jobDir := os.Getenv("CLAUDE_JOB_DIR"); jobDir != "" {
    short := filepath.Base(jobDir)   // CLAUDE_JOB_DIR ends in /<short_id> per research §6
    // UPDATE sessions SET session_id=<payload.SessionID>, last_activity=now
    // WHERE short_id=<short> AND kind='background'
    // If no row matches → log and fall through to normal new-row path
    // (defensive: the dispatch row should exist, but don't crash if it doesn't).
    return
}
```

The hook skips the worktree-cwd auto-bind path for bg context (bg agents don't have a meaningful cwd for binding). After this UPDATE, the bg row has both `session_id` (real UUID) and `short_id` (dispatch handle), enabling FK links from `session_messages` to actually attach the bg agent's transcript.

### Status of target task
Same gate as `endless task spawn` (fg path): `--force` required for done-ish, `--reopen` reopens terminal. Bg adds nothing here — reuse the existing gate code.

### Throttling
Out of scope for E-1568 — that's **E-1572**. This child unconditionally dispatches.

### Window-name / status-line
No fg tmux window is created for bg agents. The coordinator's window keeps its name. The fg coordinator session's `active_epic_id` is left as-is (the user manages focus separately via `endless task attach` or `endless task spawn --attach`, both delivered by E-1570).

### No schema-change file in this child
All schema work is consolidated in **E-1571**'s change file. E-1568 only reads/writes the columns E-1571 added.

## Files

### Changed
- `src/endless/task_cmd.py`:
  - `spawn_plan(...)` gains `bg: bool = False` and a `--bg` click option declaration.
  - Branch at the post-pre-claim point: if `bg`, skip tmux new-window / send-keys flow, instead build `claude --bg --name f"E-{item_id}"` argv + handoff as positional, `subprocess.run(capture_output=True, cwd=<cd_target>)` where `cd_target` is the worktree (overridden by `--worktree` if supplied).
  - Parse short id from stdout (regex `r"^backgrounded · ([a-f0-9]+) · "` with `re.M`). On miss, raise ClickException with stdout.
  - Resolve `active_epic_id`: helper `_resolve_epic_ancestor(task_id) -> int | None` that walks parents; returns the nearest type=epic ancestor's id.
  - Write the sessions row via the existing event-bridge path (or a new `monitor.RecordBgAgentSession` Go helper — pick the same path other Python session writes use; locate via `grep -n "INTO sessions" src/endless/` at implementation). The helper accepts a nullable `session_id` (NULL at dispatch).
  - Echo `Backgrounded E-{item_id} as <short_id>. Attach with: endless task attach E-{item_id}` (the verb comes from E-1570; the line is forward-compatible — until E-1570 lands the user can still `claude attach <short_id>` manually).
- `internal/hookcmd/claude.go::handleSessionStart` — bg-context decoration branch as described above.
- `internal/monitor/sessions.go` (whichever file holds inserts) — `RecordBgAgentSession(taskID, projectID int64, activeEpicID *int64, shortID string)` helper (returns the inserted id). Accepts NULL `session_id`.

### Tests
- `tests/test_spawn_bg.py` (new):
  - Mock subprocess; assert `claude --bg --name "E-N"` invoked with rendered handoff and correct `cwd`.
  - Capture short id from canned stdout; assert sessions row written with `session_id=NULL`, `short_id=<short>`, `kind='background'`, `active_task_id=<id>`, `active_epic_id=<epic>` or NULL.
  - `active_epic_id` resolution: bg-dispatch a child of an epic → row has parent epic id. Bg-dispatch a task with no epic ancestor → NULL.
  - `--bg --worktree /tmp` → subprocess invoked with `cwd=/tmp`; no error.
  - On parse-failure (canned stdout without the "backgrounded" line) → ClickException raised, no sessions row written.
- `internal/hookcmd/claude_bg_session_test.go` (new):
  - Set `CLAUDE_JOB_DIR=/tmp/test/abc1234`, insert a sessions row with `short_id='abc1234'` + `session_id=NULL` + `kind='background'`, fire SessionStart with source=startup and a fake UUID, assert: existing row UPDATEd with `session_id=<UUID>`, `last_activity` bumped, no new row inserted.
  - Same setup but no matching short_id row → falls through to normal SessionStart path (defensive).
- Regex unit test for the short-id parser (covers extra trailing whitespace, alternate ID lengths, ANSI codes if any).

## Sequence note

Lands after **E-1565, E-1566, E-1571, E-1569**. Required by **E-1570** (attach verbs read `short_id`) and **E-1572** (throttle counts `kind='background'` rows).

## Verification

1. `just build && just test` pass.
2. From a worktree-bound session, claim an epic, then `endless task spawn --bg <child-id>` → command returns near-instant, prints short id, `endless sql "SELECT session_id, kind, active_task_id, active_epic_id, short_id FROM sessions ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1" --db main` shows the row with `session_id IS NULL`, `kind='background'`, and `short_id` populated.
3. Wait a few seconds for the bg agent's `SessionStart` to fire; re-query → `session_id` now populated with the real UUID, `short_id` unchanged.
4. `claude agents` (the Claude CLI) lists the bg agent with name `E-<task_id>`.
5. Sanity check: `claude attach <short_id>` from a fresh terminal drops into the bg agent (this dovetails with E-1570).
6. `endless task spawn --bg --worktree /tmp <id>` → bg agent dispatches with cwd=/tmp; row written; no error.

# E-1570 — Add endless task spawn --attach and endless task attach for bg-agent viewing

Lands after **E-1568** (bg dispatch writes `short_id`). Two attach verbs.

## Decisions

### `endless task spawn --attach <id>` — new tmux window
- **Modifier on spawn**, not a dispatcher. Requires the target task to already have a `kind='background'` row in `sessions` with a populated `short_id`. Does NOT dispatch a new bg agent.
- Mutually exclusive with `--bg` (you either dispatch or attach, not both — chaining is "spawn --bg then spawn --attach", explicit two calls).
- Flow:
  1. Lookup: `SELECT short_id FROM sessions WHERE active_task_id=? AND kind='background' AND state='working' ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1`. If no row → ClickException: *"E-{id} has no live bg agent. Dispatch with `endless task spawn --bg E-{id}` first."*
  2. Build window name with existing `_spawn_window_name(...)` helper (so the window is recognizable as "this task's attach window").
  3. `tmux new-window -n <window_name>`.
  4. Optionally `tmux set -w -t <window_name> @endless_attached_short_id <short_id>` for diagnostics — not load-bearing.
  5. `tmux send-keys -t <window_name> "claude attach <short_id>" Enter`.
  6. Print return-line hint (the user can detach with `←` / `Ctrl+Z`, which the research doc documents).

### `endless task attach <id>` — exec current pane
- New top-level subcommand under `endless task` (no `spawn` prefix — this is its own verb, not a flag).
- Replaces the current process with `claude attach <short_id>` via `os.execvp`. Documented as "use from a fresh shell" — if used from inside an active Claude/coordinator pane, the user kills their own coordinator. We do **not** add a confirm prompt; explicit verb name carries the meaning.
- Flow:
  1. Same DB lookup as above. Error message tailored: *"E-{id} has no live bg agent."*
  2. If `os.environ.get("CLAUDECODE") == "1"` (the documented Claude-spawned subprocess marker, research doc §6) → warn loudly to stderr: *"You are inside a Claude session. `endless task attach` replaces the current process; you will lose this session. Re-run with --force to proceed, or open a fresh terminal."* Then exit non-zero unless `--force`.
  3. Otherwise `os.execvp("claude", ["claude", "attach", short_id])`. No return — process is replaced.

### Resolving "current view" for status-line
When the coordinator runs `task attach E-CHILD`, the coordinator's *separate* fg session is unaffected. The newly-attached pane is a fresh process whose own SessionStart hook fires. That hook's bg-context detection (added in E-1568) recognizes `CLAUDE_JOB_DIR`, finds the bg row, and decorates with `last_activity`. No new sessions row gets inserted.

For `spawn --attach`, the new tmux window's pane *also* runs `claude attach` — same hook path, same outcome.

The coordinator's `sessions.active_task_id` only changes when the **user explicitly** runs `endless task claim` from the coordinator pane (existing flow). E-1570 doesn't tweak active_task_id implicitly.

### `claude attach` resolution
Reuse the binary resolution from `spawn_plan` (lines 3398–3400): `~/.local/bin/claude` if present, else `claude`. Extract to a small helper `_claude_binary()` so both spawn and attach call it — drive-by refactor, isolated.

## Files

### Changed
- `src/endless/task_cmd.py`:
  - `spawn_plan` gains `attach: bool = False` and a `--attach` click option. Validate mutual exclusion with `--bg`. Branch at the top — if `attach`, run the attach flow (lookup, tmux new-window, send-keys) and return; skip all pre-claim/dispatch logic.
  - New click command `task_attach(item_id, force)` registered as `task_cmd.command("attach")`. Implements the exec flow.
  - New helper `_claude_binary() -> str` (extract from `spawn_plan`).
  - New helper `_lookup_bg_short_id(task_id: int) -> str | None` — single source of truth for the SELECT, reused by both attach verbs.

### Tests
- `tests/test_task_attach.py` (new):
  - `task attach` with no bg row → exits non-zero with message.
  - `task attach` from CLAUDECODE=1 env → refuses without --force, accepts with --force (mock `os.execvp`).
  - `task attach` happy path → `os.execvp` called with `("claude", "claude", "attach", "<short_id>")`.
  - `task spawn --attach` with no bg row → exits non-zero.
  - `task spawn --attach` happy path → mocks subprocess; asserts `tmux new-window` then `tmux send-keys ... "claude attach <short>"`.
  - `task spawn --bg --attach` → mutual-exclusion error.

## Sequence note

Depends on **E-1568** (short_id column populated). Independent of E-1567 / E-1572 / E-1573.

## Verification

1. `just test` passes.
2. End-to-end: claim epic, `spawn --bg <child>`, `spawn --attach <child>` → new tmux window opens running the bg agent.
3. Detach with `Ctrl+Z`; the bg agent keeps running (`claude agents --json` still lists it).
4. From a fresh terminal: `endless task attach E-{child}` drops you into the bg agent in-place.
5. Inside the coordinator session: `endless task attach E-{child}` refuses unless `--force`.

# E-1571 — Add sessions.active_epic_id column and kind discriminator for bg-agent rows

Lands after E-1565 / E-1566 (per-type templates, not directly dependent) and **before** E-1568 (--bg dispatch writes these columns) and E-1570 (attach verbs may read kind to discriminate fg vs bg).

## Decisions

### Schema additions
Two new columns on `sessions`:

- `active_epic_id INTEGER` — nullable FK to `tasks(id)`. Semantics:
  - **Coordinator (epic) fg row:** stable = the epic's task id; `active_task_id` mutates as user switches view to children.
  - **Bg-agent row:** `active_task_id` = the child task id; `active_epic_id` = parent epic id (resolved at spawn time by Python).
  - **Non-epic fg row:** NULL.
- `kind TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'tmux'` — discriminator: `'tmux'` or `'background'`. App-level validation (no CHECK, per house rule). Backfill: all existing rows become `'tmux'`.

`process` column (the tmux pane id) is already nullable in the current schema. No structural change there — semantics shift: `kind='background'` rows legitimately leave it NULL. Document the invariant in a comment.

### Window-name / status-line rendering
Status-line `[E-NNN]` prefix in `internal/tmuxcmd/status_line.go::format()` (line 69) becomes:

- `active_epic_id IS NULL` → `[E-NNNN]` (today's behavior, unchanged)
- `active_epic_id IS NOT NULL AND active_epic_id = active_task_id` → `[E-EEEE]` (viewing the epic itself)
- `active_epic_id IS NOT NULL AND active_epic_id != active_task_id` → `[E-EEEE:E-CCCC]` (viewing a child of the epic)

Same logic applied to `internal/tmuxcmd/show_menu.go::format()` (line 75) for the popup header.

`monitor.ActiveTaskInfo` (the struct the format functions read) gains `ActiveEpicID *int64`. The query that populates it joins `sessions` (it already does — that's where `active_task_id` comes from) and selects `active_epic_id`.

### Coordinator row vs child-view: how active_task_id moves
Out of scope here — this child only adds the columns. The mechanism for "user switches focus to a child" is part of E-1570 (attach verbs) and the day-to-day `endless task claim` flow. This child does NOT implement focus-switching; it only ensures the data shape exists.

### "Focused bg agent" derivation
Per E-1552 outcome ("derived from the coordinator row, not stored"), no column added. Queries that need it compute it: `SELECT * FROM sessions WHERE kind='background' AND active_task_id = <coordinator's active_task_id>` — i.e. the bg agent whose child task matches what the coordinator is viewing. This child adds a Go helper `monitor.FocusedBgAgent(coordinatorSessionID) (*Session, error)` so callers don't reinvent the SQL.

### Validation
Go-side validator (`internal/monitor/sessions.go` or wherever existing validation lives — locate via grep at implementation): reject any insert/update where `kind NOT IN ('tmux','background')`. Mirrors the TaskType pattern (E-1538). No SQL CHECK.

Startup integrity check warns (not fails) if a `kind='background'` row has `process IS NOT NULL` or if a `kind='tmux'` row has `process IS NULL` while `state='working'` — the schema doesn't enforce these, but drift is interesting.

## Files

### New
- `internal/schema/changes/e-1571-sessions-active-epic-and-kind.sql` — change file for `just land`:
  ```sql
  ALTER TABLE sessions ADD COLUMN active_epic_id INTEGER REFERENCES tasks(id);
  ALTER TABLE sessions ADD COLUMN kind TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'tmux';
  -- No backfill UPDATE needed: existing rows take the default 'tmux'.
  ```

### Changed
- `internal/schema/schema.sql` — declare the two new columns on the `CREATE TABLE sessions IF NOT EXISTS` block. Add the FK clause for `active_epic_id` analogous to existing `active_task_id` FK. Add a comment block above the new columns explaining the kind discriminator and active_epic_id semantics.
- `internal/monitor/sessions.go` (or whichever file holds session CRUD — locate at impl): expose `ActiveEpicID *int64` and `Kind string` on the Go session struct, include in SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE queries, validate `kind` on write.
- `internal/monitor/tmux.go` — extend `ActiveTaskInfo` with `ActiveEpicID *int64`, populate from query.
- `internal/tmuxcmd/status_line.go::format()` — branch on `info.ActiveEpicID`.
- `internal/tmuxcmd/show_menu.go::format()` — same branch.
- `internal/monitor/sessions_focus.go` (new file is fine, or fold into existing) — `FocusedBgAgent(coordinatorSessionID)` helper.

### Tests
- `internal/monitor/sessions_test.go` — round-trip a row with `kind='background'` + `active_epic_id` set; reject `kind='other'`.
- `internal/tmuxcmd/status_line_test.go` (or wherever format is tested) — three cases for the prefix render.
- `internal/monitor/sessions_focus_test.go` — `FocusedBgAgent` returns the right row given a coordinator + several children + matching/non-matching bg agents.

## Sequence note

Lands before **E-1568** (which writes `kind='background'` + `active_epic_id` rows) and **E-1570** (attach verbs may query `kind` to validate target).

## Verification

1. `just build` succeeds; `just land` applies the change file cleanly against a populated DB (test via sandbox: `endless-sandbox init e-test --mode populated`, then `endless db apply-change e-1571-sessions-active-epic-and-kind`).
2. `just test` passes (Go).

### Schema-change file
E-1571 shipped with `active_epic_id` + `kind` only. This child adds:

- `short_id TEXT` — generic across harnesses (Claude's ~8-hex handle today; future Codex/OpenCode equivalents reuse the same column). The harness discriminator is the existing `sessions.platform` column; format is harness-determined. UNIQUE via partial index (only enforced where NOT NULL).
- `session_id` constraint relaxed: drop `NOT NULL`. Bg rows are inserted with `session_id = NULL` at dispatch; SessionStart UPDATEs to the real UUID when the bg agent's hook fires. UNIQUE constraint preserved.

SQLite has no `ALTER COLUMN DROP NOT NULL`; the change file performs a full table rebuild in a single transaction with `PRAGMA foreign_keys = OFF`:

`internal/schema/changes/e-1568-sessions-short-id-and-nullable-session-id.sql`:

```sql
PRAGMA foreign_keys = OFF;
BEGIN;

CREATE TABLE sessions_new (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    session_id TEXT,                         -- was NOT NULL
    project_id INTEGER,
    platform TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'claude',
    state TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'working',
    active_task_id INTEGER,
    plan_file_path TEXT,
    process TEXT,
    started_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S', 'now')),
    last_activity TEXT,
    transcript_offset INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    transcript_path TEXT,
    summary TEXT,
    hidden INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    needs_recap INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    summary_seq INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    active_epic_id INTEGER,
    kind TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'tmux',
    short_id TEXT,                           -- new
    UNIQUE (session_id),
    FOREIGN KEY (project_id) REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    FOREIGN KEY (active_task_id) REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    FOREIGN KEY (active_epic_id) REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE SET NULL
);

INSERT INTO sessions_new (id, session_id, project_id, platform, state, active_task_id,
    plan_file_path, process, started_at, last_activity, transcript_offset,
    transcript_path, summary, hidden, needs_recap, summary_seq, active_epic_id, kind)
SELECT id, session_id, project_id, platform, state, active_task_id,
    plan_file_path, process, started_at, last_activity, transcript_offset,
    transcript_path, summary, hidden, needs_recap, summary_seq, active_epic_id, kind
FROM sessions;

DROP TABLE sessions;
ALTER TABLE sessions_new RENAME TO sessions;

CREATE TRIGGER sessions_null_process_on_end_update
AFTER UPDATE OF state ON sessions
WHEN NEW.state = 'ended' AND NEW.process IS NOT NULL
BEGIN
    UPDATE sessions SET process = NULL WHERE id = NEW.id;
END;

CREATE TRIGGER sessions_null_process_on_end_insert
AFTER INSERT ON sessions
WHEN NEW.state = 'ended' AND NEW.process IS NOT NULL
BEGIN
    UPDATE sessions SET process = NULL WHERE id = NEW.id;
END;

CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_sessions_short_id_unique
    ON sessions(short_id) WHERE short_id IS NOT NULL;

COMMIT;
PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;
```

FKs from other tables (`session_messages.session_id`, `session_gates.session_id` referencing `sessions(session_id)`) resolve by name post-RENAME. The `foreign_keys = OFF` block prevents transient violations during the swap.

Mirror the post-migration shape in `internal/schema/schema.sql`: `session_id TEXT` (no NOT NULL) + `UNIQUE`; add `short_id TEXT`; add the partial unique index.

## Files

### New
- `internal/schema/changes/e-1568-sessions-short-id-and-nullable-session-id.sql` — table rebuild above.

- `internal/schema/schema.sql` — declare post-migration shape: `session_id TEXT` (drop NOT NULL) + `UNIQUE`; add `short_id TEXT`; add partial unique index `idx_sessions_short_id_unique`. Comment block explaining `short_id`'s harness-agnostic role (paired with the existing `platform` column).
- `src/endless/task_cmd.py`:
  - `spawn_plan(...)` gains `bg: bool = False` and a `--bg` click option declaration.
  - Branch at the post-pre-claim point: if `bg`, skip tmux new-window / send-keys flow, instead build `claude --bg --name f"E-{item_id}"` argv + handoff as positional, `subprocess.run(capture_output=True, cwd=<cd_target>)` where `cd_target` is the worktree (overridden by `--worktree` if supplied).
  - Parse short id from stdout (regex `r"^backgrounded · ([a-f0-9]+) · "` with `re.M`). On miss, raise ClickException with stdout.
  - Resolve `active_epic_id`: helper `_resolve_epic_ancestor(task_id) -> int | None` that walks parents; returns the nearest type=epic ancestor's id.
  - Write the sessions row via the existing event-bridge path (or a new `monitor.RecordBgAgentSession` Go helper — pick the same path other Python session writes use; locate via `grep -n "INTO sessions" src/endless/` at implementation). The helper accepts a nullable `session_id` (NULL at dispatch).
  - Echo `Backgrounded E-{item_id} as <short_id>. Attach with: endless task attach E-{item_id}` (the verb comes from E-1570; the line is forward-compatible — until E-1570 lands the user can still `claude attach <short_id>` manually).
- `internal/hookcmd/claude.go::handleSessionStart` — bg-context decoration branch as described above.
- `internal/monitor/sessions.go` (whichever file holds inserts) — `RecordBgAgentSession(taskID, projectID int64, activeEpicID *int64, shortID string)` helper (returns the inserted id). Accepts NULL `session_id`.

### Tests
- `tests/test_spawn_bg.py` (new):
  - Mock subprocess; assert `claude --bg --name "E-N"` invoked with rendered handoff and correct `cwd`.
  - Capture short id from canned stdout; assert sessions row written with `session_id=NULL`, `short_id=<short>`, `kind='background'`, `active_task_id=<id>`, `active_epic_id=<epic>` or NULL.
  - `active_epic_id` resolution: bg-dispatch a child of an epic → row has parent epic id. Bg-dispatch a task with no epic ancestor → NULL.
  - `--bg --worktree /tmp` → subprocess invoked with `cwd=/tmp`; no error.
  - On parse-failure (canned stdout without the "backgrounded" line) → ClickException raised, no sessions row written.
- `internal/hookcmd/claude_bg_session_test.go` (new):
  - Set `CLAUDE_JOB_DIR=/tmp/test/abc1234`, insert a sessions row with `short_id='abc1234'` + `session_id=NULL` + `kind='background'`, fire SessionStart with source=startup and a fake UUID, assert: existing row UPDATEd with `session_id=<UUID>`, `last_activity` bumped, no new row inserted.
  - Same setup but no matching short_id row → falls through to normal SessionStart path (defensive).
- Regex unit test for the short-id parser (covers extra trailing whitespace, alternate ID lengths, ANSI codes if any).

## Sequence note

Lands after **E-1565, E-1566, E-1571, E-1569**. Required by **E-1570** (attach verbs read `short_id`) and **E-1572** (throttle counts `kind='background'` rows).

## Verification

1. `just build && just test` pass.
2. `just land` applies `e-1568-sessions-short-id-and-nullable-session-id` cleanly against a populated DB (test via sandbox: `endless-sandbox init e-test --mode populated`, then `endless db apply-change e-1568-sessions-short-id-and-nullable-session-id`). SQL probes: `SELECT "notnull" FROM pragma_table_info('sessions') WHERE name='session_id';` returns `0`; `short_id` column present; `idx_sessions_short_id_unique` present; both `sessions_null_process_*` triggers present.
3. From a worktree-bound session, claim an epic, then `endless task spawn --bg <child-id>` → command returns near-instant, prints short id, `endless sql "SELECT session_id, kind, active_task_id, active_epic_id, short_id FROM sessions ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1" --db main` shows the row with `session_id IS NULL`, `kind='background'`, and `short_id` populated.
4. Wait a few seconds for the bg agent's `SessionStart` to fire; re-query → `session_id` now populated with the real UUID, `short_id` unchanged.
5. `claude agents` (the Claude CLI) lists the bg agent with name `E-<task_id>`.
6. Sanity check: `claude attach <short_id>` from a fresh terminal drops into the bg agent (this dovetails with E-1570).
7. `endless task spawn --bg --worktree /tmp <id>` → bg agent dispatches with cwd=/tmp; row written; no error.

payload.title: Document esu shell helper in endless guide's verify-handoff section
payload.description: Extend endless guide step 5 (verify-handoff) to instruct spawned sessions to open their 'To verify:' command block with `esu` rather than an absolute `cd /path/to/worktree`. The guide already mentions `esu` in 'Get into the worktree' (step 3) but doesn't carry it forward into the verify-handoff guidance — so sessions like E-1577 default to writing absolute paths in the verify block, which forces Mike to re-type. Higher-leverage than the per-template fix because every spawned session reads the guide on step 1.

payload.title: Create per-task verification scripts under tests/tasks/ starting with E-1577
payload.description: Formalize the verify-handoff process: a verification SCRIPT per task in tests/tasks/ that runs the verify checks and reports ALL PASSED or a list of failures with detail Mike and the spawning session can diagnose from. Script is committed to version control, optionally reviewable by Mike before running, and ultimately part of repeatable testing. Long-term: subagents/Codex generate the scripts, adversarial agents review them for coverage. First deliverable: an ad-hoc example for E-1577 (e.g. tests/tasks/e-1577-verify.sh) plus the convention/framework so future spawned sessions produce one for every verify-handoff. Pickup session should INTERVIEW Mike on framework shape (output format, assertion style, DB sandbox setup, runner integration with `just test`, naming/location) BEFORE planning. DO NOT plan until the interview is complete.

fields.text:
# E-1568 — Add --bg flag to endless task spawn that dispatches via claude --bg --name carrier

Lands after **E-1565** (renderer), **E-1566** (type variants), **E-1571** (sessions: kind + active_epic_id), and **E-1569** (bgIsolation:'none' in settings). All four are landed (`assumed`). This is the centerpiece of the epic.

> **Plan corrected at pickup (2026-06-18)** against the real tree. Three deviations from the
> original draft, two adapted to reality and one decided with Mike:
> 1. E-1571 shipped `kind_id INTEGER` (FK → `session_kinds`, `1=tmux`/`2=background`), **not** a
>    `kind TEXT` column. Every `kind='background'` below is `kind_id=2`.
> 2. `session_gates` was dropped by `e-1582`; the only remaining FK to `sessions(session_id)` is
>    `session_messages` (ON DELETE CASCADE). Other FKs point at `sessions(id)`.
> 3. The schema change is a **`.go` change file** (runner, FK OFF), not `.sql`. The `.sql`
>    dispatcher runs on `monitor.DB()` (FK ON) inside `BEGIN IMMEDIATE`, where `PRAGMA
>    foreign_keys` is a no-op — a table rebuild can't work there. E-1571 hit this exact wall.
>
> Decisions confirmed with Mike: (a) **rebuild + nullable session_id** (not a placeholder hack);
> (b) **`{{if .bg}}` branch in the 4 per-type handoff templates** (not new files, not reuse-as-is).

## Decisions

### CLI surface
- New flag `--bg` on `endless task spawn <id>`.
- `--worktree <path>` works in both fg and bg: in bg it sets `subprocess.run(..., cwd=<path>)`.
  Default remains the spawn-created worktree `.endless/worktrees/e-<id>/`.
- Compatible with `--reopen`, `--force`. `--no-plan` is a no-op for bg (no `/plan` slash concept) — document and ignore.
- Compatible with `--type` of the target task (renderer picks the right handoff variant).
- **tmux gate is skipped for `--bg`.** The fg path requires `$TMUX`; bg agents are headless, so
  the `not os.environ.get("TMUX")` guard is bypassed when `bg=True`.

### Invocation
Per `docs/research-2026-06-12-claude-background-agents.md` §2/§7:
```bash
claude --bg --name "E-<task_id>" "<rendered-handoff-text>"
```
Prompt is positional argv (handoff ~2 KB, well under Darwin ARG_MAX 256 KB). Stdout returns a
human-formatted block whose first line is `backgrounded · <8hex> · E-<task_id>` (no ANSI codes per §2).

### Short-ID capture
Parse the first stdout line matching `^backgrounded · ([a-f0-9]+) · ` (`re.M`, regex pinned in code
with comment + research-doc citation). On no-match, raise `ClickException` with the raw stdout —
never silently proceed with a missing id.

### Session row write (Go, not Python)
Per E-1486 (no new Python SQLite access), the row is written by a **new Go helper invoked via an
`endless-go` subcommand** — mirroring how `render_handoff` and `session-query ensure-claude-id`
already shell out. Python passes `task_id`, `short_id`; Go resolves `project_id` and the epic
ancestor itself and INSERTs:

| Column | Value |
|---|---|
| `session_id` | **NULL** — bg agents have no UUID at dispatch. SessionStart UPDATEs it later. UNIQUE treats NULL as distinct, so multiple pending bg rows coexist. |
| `project_id` | resolved from the task row |
| `platform` | `'claude'` |
| `state` | `'working'` |
| `active_task_id` | `<task_id>` |
| `active_epic_id` | nearest `type='epic'` ancestor via `WITH RECURSIVE` walk up `tasks.parent_id` (joined to `task_types`); NULL if none. **Resolved in Go**, not Python. |
| `kind_id` | `2` (`background`) |
| `process` | NULL |
| `short_id` | `<short_id>` from dispatch stdout |
| `started_at` | now |

`short_id` is the durable handle: `claude attach <short_id>` works; the row survives the UUID arriving later.

### SessionStart hook update (bg-context decoration)
New branch in `internal/hookcmd/claude.go` SessionStart handling, BEFORE the spawn-marker / cwd
auto-bind paths (bg agents have no meaningful tmux marker or binding cwd):

```go
if jobDir := os.Getenv("CLAUDE_JOB_DIR"); jobDir != "" {
    short := filepath.Base(jobDir)   // CLAUDE_JOB_DIR = ~/.claude/jobs/<short_id> per research §6
    // UPDATE sessions SET session_id=?, last_activity=? WHERE short_id=? AND kind_id=2
    // rows==0 → log + fall through to normal new-row path (defensive)
    // rows>=1 → return (decorated; skip auto-bind)
}
```
Uses the same `monitor.DB()` + parameterized `db.Exec` pattern as the other session UPDATEs.
A new `monitor.DecorateBgSession(shortID, sessionID string) (int64, error)` helper returns rows-affected.

### Status gate / throttling / window-name
Same done-ish gate as fg (`--force`/`--reopen` reuse existing code). Throttling is E-1572 — out of
scope; this child unconditionally dispatches. No fg tmux window; coordinator window keeps its name.

### Schema change — `.go` rebuild
`internal/schema/changes/e-1568-sessions-short-id-and-nullable-session-id.go` (mirrors the
`//go:build ignore` + `runner.Run` shape of `e-1571-...go`). One transaction, runner connection has
FK OFF:

1. `CREATE TABLE sessions_new (...)` — identical column set/order to current `sessions` **plus**
   `short_id TEXT`, with `session_id TEXT` (NOT NULL dropped), `UNIQUE (session_id)`, and the
   existing FKs (`project_id`, `active_task_id`, `active_epic_id` → tasks; `kind_id` → session_kinds).
2. `INSERT INTO sessions_new (<all existing cols>) SELECT <same> FROM sessions` (short_id defaults NULL).
3. `DROP TABLE sessions; ALTER TABLE sessions_new RENAME TO sessions`.
4. Recreate both `sessions_null_process_on_end_{update,insert}` triggers (DROP TABLE took them).
5. `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_sessions_short_id_unique ON sessions(short_id) WHERE short_id IS NOT NULL`.

`session_messages` FK (→ `sessions(session_id)`) re-resolves by name post-RENAME; FK-OFF prevents
transient CASCADE during the swap.

Mirror the post-change shape in `internal/schema/schema.sql`: drop `NOT NULL` on `session_id`
(keep `UNIQUE`), add `short_id TEXT` (comment: harness-agnostic handle paired with `platform`), add
the partial unique index.

### bg handoff templates
`render_handoff` gains a `bg: bool = False` param, passed into `vars_payload["bg"]`. Each of
`handoff/{task,bug,research,epic}.md.tmpl` gets a `{{if .bg}} … {{else}} … {{end}}` split around the
tmux-specific sections: the bg branch omits the `tmux switch-client` / `tmux move-window -t archive`
return lines and the "return to that session" framing, and instead tells the agent to do the work,
flip the task to `verify`, and stop (the user attaches later via `claude attach <short_id>`).

## Files

### New
- `internal/schema/changes/e-1568-sessions-short-id-and-nullable-session-id.go` — rebuild above.
- `tests/test_spawn_bg.py` — Python bg-dispatch tests.
- `internal/hookcmd/claude_bg_session_test.go` — SessionStart bg-decoration tests.

### Modified
- `internal/schema/schema.sql` — nullable `session_id`, add `short_id` + partial unique index.
- `src/endless/task_cmd.py` — `spawn_plan(..., bg=False)`; skip tmux gate for bg; bg branch after
  pre-claim (build `claude --bg --name E-<id>` argv + handoff positional, `subprocess.run(cwd=...)`);
  parse short id; call new `endless-go` subcommand to write the row; echo
  `Backgrounded E-<id> as <short_id>. Attach: claude attach <short_id>`. `render_handoff` gains `bg`.
- `src/endless/cli.py` — `--bg` click option on the `spawn` command; pass through to `spawn_plan`.
- `internal/monitor/session.go` — `RecordBgAgentSession(taskID int64, shortID string) (int64, error)`
  (resolves project_id + epic ancestor, inserts with `session_id=NULL`, `kind_id=2`) and
  `DecorateBgSession(shortID, sessionID string) (int64, error)`.
- `internal/hookcmd/claude.go` — CLAUDE_JOB_DIR bg-decoration branch.
- new `endless-go` subcommand wiring (e.g. `session-query record-bg-agent --task-id --short-id`)
  calling `RecordBgAgentSession`; locate the existing `session-query` command group and add the verb.
- `internal/templatecmd/templates/handoff/{task,bug,research,epic}.md.tmpl` — `{{if .bg}}` branch.

### Tests
- `tests/test_spawn_bg.py`: mock subprocess; assert `claude --bg --name E-N <handoff>` + correct `cwd`;
  parse short id from canned stdout; assert the row-write subcommand is invoked with the right args;
  `--bg --worktree /tmp` → `cwd=/tmp`; parse-failure → ClickException, no row write.
- `internal/monitor/*_test.go`: `RecordBgAgentSession` writes `session_id=NULL`, `short_id`,
  `kind_id=2`, `active_task_id`, and `active_epic_id` = parent epic (or NULL when no epic ancestor).
- `internal/hookcmd/claude_bg_session_test.go`: `CLAUDE_JOB_DIR=.../abc1234` + a bg row with
  `short_id='abc1234'`, `session_id=NULL`, `kind_id=2` → SessionStart (source=startup, fake UUID)
  UPDATEs that row's `session_id` + `last_activity`, no new row. No-match short_id → falls through.
- Regex unit test for the short-id parser (trailing whitespace, alternate id lengths).

## Verification

1. `just build && just test` pass.
2. Sandbox migration: `endless-sandbox init e-test --mode populated`, then
   `endless db apply-change internal/schema/changes/e-1568-sessions-short-id-and-nullable-session-id.go`.
   Probes: `SELECT "notnull" FROM pragma_table_info('sessions') WHERE name='session_id';` → `0`;
   `short_id` column present; `idx_sessions_short_id_unique` present; both `sessions_null_process_*`
   triggers present; `kind_id` FK intact.
3. From a worktree-bound session, claim an epic, `endless task spawn --bg <child-id>` → returns
   near-instant, prints short id; `endless sql "SELECT session_id, kind_id, active_task_id,
   active_epic_id, short_id FROM sessions ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1" --db main` shows
   `session_id IS NULL`, `kind_id=2`, `short_id` populated, `active_epic_id` = the epic.
4. Wait for the bg agent's SessionStart; re-query → `session_id` now the real UUID, `short_id` unchanged.
5. `claude agents` lists the bg agent named `E-<task_id>`; `claude attach <short_id>` drops in.
6. `endless task spawn --bg --worktree /tmp <id>` → bg dispatches with cwd=/tmp; row written; no error.

## Sequence note
Required by **E-1570** (attach verbs read `short_id`) and **E-1572** (throttle counts `kind_id=2` rows).

payload.title: Support handoff templates keyed on both task type and ground (fg/bg) dimensions

fields.description: E-1568 added a single `{{if .bg}}` conditional inside each per-type handoff template (task/bug/research/epic) to strip tmux return lines for background agents. If background handoffs need to diverge further per type, replace the inline conditional with a proper type x ground (fg/bg) template matrix so each combination can be authored independently.

fields.text:
# E-1570 — Add endless task spawn --attach and endless task attach for bg-agent viewing

Lands after **E-1568** (bg dispatch writes `short_id`). Two attach verbs.

## Decisions

### `endless task spawn --attach <id>` — new tmux window
- **Modifier on spawn**, not a dispatcher. Requires the target task to already have a `kind='background'` row in `sessions` with a populated `short_id`. Does NOT dispatch a new bg agent.
- Mutually exclusive with `--bg` (you either dispatch or attach, not both — chaining is "spawn --bg then spawn --attach", explicit two calls).
- Flow:
  1. Lookup: `SELECT short_id FROM sessions WHERE active_task_id=? AND kind='background' AND state='working' ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1`. If no row → ClickException: *"E-{id} has no live bg agent. Dispatch with `endless task spawn --bg E-{id}` first."*
  2. Build window name with existing `_spawn_window_name(...)` helper (so the window is recognizable as "this task's attach window").
  3. `tmux new-window -n <window_name>`.
  4. Optionally `tmux set -w -t <window_name> @endless_attached_short_id <short_id>` for diagnostics — not load-bearing.
  5. `tmux send-keys -t <window_name> "claude attach <short_id>" Enter`.
  6. Print return-line hint (the user can detach with `←` / `Ctrl+Z`, which the research doc documents).

### `endless task attach <id>` — exec current pane
- New top-level subcommand under `endless task` (no `spawn` prefix — this is its own verb, not a flag).
- Replaces the current process with `claude attach <short_id>` via `os.execvp`. Documented as "use from a fresh shell" — if used from inside an active Claude/coordinator pane, the user kills their own coordinator. We do **not** add a confirm prompt; explicit verb name carries the meaning.
- **Go-port note:** when this verb migrates from Python to Go, substitute `syscall.Exec(path, []string{"claude", "attach", shortID}, os.Environ())` after `exec.LookPath("claude")`. POSIX `execve` semantics are identical; only PATH lookup is now explicit. No design change.
- Flow:
  1. Same DB lookup as above. Error message tailored: *"E-{id} has no live bg agent."*
  2. If `os.environ.get("CLAUDECODE") == "1"` (the documented Claude-spawned subprocess marker, research doc §6) → warn loudly to stderr: *"You are inside a Claude session. `endless task attach` replaces the current process; you will lose this session. Re-run with --force to proceed, or open a fresh terminal."* Then exit non-zero unless `--force`.
  3. Otherwise `os.execvp("claude", ["claude", "attach", short_id])`. No return — process is replaced.

### Resolving "current view" for status-line
When the coordinator runs `task attach E-CHILD`, the coordinator's *separate* fg session is unaffected. The newly-attached pane is a fresh process whose own SessionStart hook fires. That hook's bg-context detection (added in E-1568) recognizes `CLAUDE_JOB_DIR`, finds the bg row, and decorates with `last_activity`. No new sessions row gets inserted.

For `spawn --attach`, the new tmux window's pane *also* runs `claude attach` — same hook path, same outcome.

The coordinator's `sessions.active_task_id` only changes when the **user explicitly** runs `endless task claim` from the coordinator pane (existing flow). E-1570 doesn't tweak active_task_id implicitly.

### `claude attach` resolution
Reuse the binary resolution from `spawn_plan` (lines 3398–3400): `~/.local/bin/claude` if present, else `claude`. Extract to a small helper `_claude_binary()` so both spawn and attach call it — drive-by refactor, isolated.

## Files

### Changed
- `src/endless/task_cmd.py`:
  - `spawn_plan` gains `attach: bool = False` and a `--attach` click option. Validate mutual exclusion with `--bg`. Branch at the top — if `attach`, run the attach flow (lookup, tmux new-window, send-keys) and return; skip all pre-claim/dispatch logic.
  - New click command `task_attach(item_id, force)` registered as `task_cmd.command("attach")`. Implements the exec flow.
  - New helper `_claude_binary() -> str` (extract from `spawn_plan`).
  - New helper `_lookup_bg_short_id(task_id: int) -> str | None` — single source of truth for the SELECT, reused by both attach verbs.

### Tests
- `tests/test_task_attach.py` (new):
  - `task attach` with no bg row → exits non-zero with message.
  - `task attach` from CLAUDECODE=1 env → refuses without --force, accepts with --force (mock `os.execvp`).
  - `task attach` happy path → `os.execvp` called with `("claude", "claude", "attach", "<short_id>")`.
  - `task spawn --attach` with no bg row → exits non-zero.
  - `task spawn --attach` happy path → mocks subprocess; asserts `tmux new-window` then `tmux send-keys ... "claude attach <short>"`.
  - `task spawn --bg --attach` → mutual-exclusion error.

## Sequence note

Depends on **E-1568** (short_id column populated). Independent of E-1567 / E-1572 / E-1573.

## Verification

1. `just test` passes.
2. End-to-end: claim epic, `spawn --bg <child>`, `spawn --attach <child>` → new tmux window opens running the bg agent.
3. Detach with `Ctrl+Z`; the bg agent keeps running (`claude agents --json` still lists it).
4. From a fresh terminal: `endless task attach E-{child}` drops you into the bg agent in-place.
5. Inside the coordinator session: `endless task attach E-{child}` refuses unless `--force`.

fields.description: Formalize the verify-handoff process: a verification SCRIPT per task in tests/tasks/ that runs the verify checks and reports ALL PASSED or a list of failures with detail Mike and the spawning session can diagnose from. Script is committed to version control, optionally reviewable by Mike before running, and ultimately part of repeatable testing. Long-term: subagents/Codex generate the scripts, adversarial agents review them for coverage. An ad-hoc prototype exists at tests/tasks/e-1577-verify.sh (written in the E-1577 session, since that session had the requirements context); treat it as an example of shape/output to learn from, NOT a deliverable of this task. Pickup session should INTERVIEW Mike on framework shape (output format, assertion style, DB sandbox setup, runner integration with `just test`, naming/location, how the convention propagates into spawn templates / endless guide so future sessions produce one for every verify-handoff) BEFORE planning. DO NOT plan until the interview is complete.

fields.text:
# E-1568 — Add --bg flag to endless task spawn that dispatches via claude --bg --name carrier

Lands after **E-1565** (renderer), **E-1566** (type variants), **E-1571** (sessions: kind + active_epic_id), and **E-1569** (bgIsolation:'none' in settings). All four are landed (`assumed`). This is the centerpiece of the epic.

> **Plan corrected at pickup (2026-06-18)** against the real tree. Three deviations from the
> original draft, two adapted to reality and one decided with Mike:
> 1. E-1571 shipped `kind_id INTEGER` (FK → `session_kinds`, `1=tmux`/`2=background`), **not** a
>    `kind TEXT` column. Every `kind='background'` below is `kind_id=2`.
> 2. `session_gates` was dropped by `e-1582`; the only remaining FK to `sessions(session_id)` is
>    `session_messages` (ON DELETE CASCADE). Other FKs point at `sessions(id)`.
> 3. The schema change is a **`.go` change file** (runner, FK OFF), not `.sql`. The `.sql`
>    dispatcher runs on `monitor.DB()` (FK ON) inside `BEGIN IMMEDIATE`, where `PRAGMA
>    foreign_keys` is a no-op — a table rebuild can't work there. E-1571 hit this exact wall.
>
> Decisions confirmed with Mike: (a) **rebuild + nullable session_id** (not a placeholder hack);
> (b) **`{{if .bg}}` branch in the 4 per-type handoff templates** (not new files, not reuse-as-is).
>
> **Correction during verify (4th finding):** the original plan's standalone
> `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX ... ON sessions(short_id) WHERE short_id IS NOT NULL` in
> schema.sql is non-deployable. `monitor.DB()` re-applies schema.sql on every
> connection — including inside `endless db apply-change`, which opens it BEFORE
> running the change file. On a pre-E-1568 DB the `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS`
> no-ops on the existing old table, so a standalone index referencing `short_id`
> errors ("no such column"), and the migration can never apply (chicken-and-egg).
> Fix: an **inline `UNIQUE (short_id)` table constraint** (same semantics —
> SQLite treats NULLs as distinct, so many NULL short_ids coexist and non-NULL
> handles stay unique) that lives inside the skipped CREATE TABLE, keeping
> schema.sql a clean no-op on old DBs. The change file's rebuilt table carries
> the same inline constraint.

## Decisions

### CLI surface
- New flag `--bg` on `endless task spawn <id>`.
- `--worktree <path>` works in both fg and bg: in bg it sets `subprocess.run(..., cwd=<path>)`.
  Default remains the spawn-created worktree `.endless/worktrees/e-<id>/`.
- Compatible with `--reopen`, `--force`. `--no-plan` is a no-op for bg (no `/plan` slash concept) — document and ignore.
- Compatible with `--type` of the target task (renderer picks the right handoff variant).
- **tmux gate is skipped for `--bg`.** The fg path requires `$TMUX`; bg agents are headless, so
  the `not os.environ.get("TMUX")` guard is bypassed when `bg=True`.

### Invocation
Per `docs/research-2026-06-12-claude-background-agents.md` §2/§7:
```bash
claude --bg --name "E-<task_id>" "<rendered-handoff-text>"
```
Prompt is positional argv (handoff ~2 KB, well under Darwin ARG_MAX 256 KB). Stdout returns a
human-formatted block whose first line is `backgrounded · <8hex> · E-<task_id>` (no ANSI codes per §2).

### Short-ID capture
Parse the first stdout line matching `^backgrounded · ([a-f0-9]+) · ` (`re.M`, regex pinned in code
with comment + research-doc citation). On no-match, raise `ClickException` with the raw stdout —
never silently proceed with a missing id.

### Session row write (Go, not Python)
Per E-1486 (no new Python SQLite access), the row is written by a **new Go helper invoked via an
`endless-go` subcommand** — mirroring how `render_handoff` and `session-query ensure-claude-id`
already shell out. Python passes `task_id`, `short_id`; Go resolves `project_id` and the epic
ancestor itself and INSERTs:

| Column | Value |
|---|---|
| `session_id` | **NULL** — bg agents have no UUID at dispatch. SessionStart UPDATEs it later. UNIQUE treats NULL as distinct, so multiple pending bg rows coexist. |
| `project_id` | resolved from the task row |
| `platform` | `'claude'` |
| `state` | `'working'` |
| `active_task_id` | `<task_id>` |
| `active_epic_id` | nearest `type='epic'` ancestor via `WITH RECURSIVE` walk up `tasks.parent_id` (joined to `task_types`); NULL if none. **Resolved in Go**, not Python. |
| `kind_id` | `2` (`background`) |
| `process` | NULL |
| `short_id` | `<short_id>` from dispatch stdout |
| `started_at` | now |

`short_id` is the durable handle: `claude attach <short_id>` works; the row survives the UUID arriving later.

### SessionStart hook update (bg-context decoration)
New branch in `internal/hookcmd/claude.go` SessionStart handling, BEFORE the spawn-marker / cwd
auto-bind paths (bg agents have no meaningful tmux marker or binding cwd):

```go
if jobDir := os.Getenv("CLAUDE_JOB_DIR"); jobDir != "" {
    short := filepath.Base(jobDir)   // CLAUDE_JOB_DIR = ~/.claude/jobs/<short_id> per research §6
    // UPDATE sessions SET session_id=?, last_activity=? WHERE short_id=? AND kind_id=2
    // rows==0 → log + fall through to normal new-row path (defensive)
    // rows>=1 → return (decorated; skip auto-bind)
}
```
Uses the same `monitor.DB()` + parameterized `db.Exec` pattern as the other session UPDATEs.
A new `monitor.DecorateBgSession(shortID, sessionID string) (int64, error)` helper returns rows-affected.

### Status gate / throttling / window-name
Same done-ish gate as fg (`--force`/`--reopen` reuse existing code). Throttling is E-1572 — out of
scope; this child unconditionally dispatches. No fg tmux window; coordinator window keeps its name.

### Schema change — `.go` rebuild
`internal/schema/changes/e-1568-sessions-short-id-and-nullable-session-id.go` (mirrors the
`//go:build ignore` + `runner.Run` shape of `e-1571-...go`). One transaction, runner connection has
FK OFF:

1. `CREATE TABLE sessions_new (...)` — identical column set/order to current `sessions` **plus**
   `short_id TEXT`, with `session_id TEXT` (NOT NULL dropped), `UNIQUE (session_id)`, and the
   existing FKs (`project_id`, `active_task_id`, `active_epic_id` → tasks; `kind_id` → session_kinds).
2. `INSERT INTO sessions_new (<all existing cols>) SELECT <same> FROM sessions` (short_id defaults NULL).
3. `DROP TABLE sessions; ALTER TABLE sessions_new RENAME TO sessions`.
4. Recreate both `sessions_null_process_on_end_{update,insert}` triggers (DROP TABLE took them).

`sessions_new` carries inline `UNIQUE (session_id)` and `UNIQUE (short_id)` (no standalone index —
see the chicken-and-egg correction above). `session_messages` FK (→ `sessions(session_id)`)
re-resolves by name post-RENAME; FK-OFF prevents transient CASCADE during the swap.

Mirror the post-change shape in `internal/schema/schema.sql`: drop `NOT NULL` on `session_id`
(keep `UNIQUE`), add `short_id TEXT` + inline `UNIQUE (short_id)` (comment: harness-agnostic handle
paired with `platform`, and why inline rather than a standalone partial index).

### bg handoff templates
`render_handoff` gains a `bg: bool = False` param, passed into `vars_payload["bg"]`. Each of
`handoff/{task,bug,research,epic}.md.tmpl` gets a `{{if .bg}} … {{else}} … {{end}}` split around the
tmux-specific sections: the bg branch omits the `tmux switch-client` / `tmux move-window -t archive`
return lines and the "return to that session" framing, and instead tells the agent to do the work,
flip the task to `verify`, and stop (the user attaches later via `claude attach <short_id>`).

## Files

### New
