- `SessionActiveEpic(sessionID int64) (*int64, error)` — literal read of `active_epic_id` (nil when NULL / no row).
- Use `sessionkind.SessionKindBackground`, not a literal 2.

### Go — `internal/sessionquerycmd/session_query.go`
- Add `list-bg-agents` case + `runListBgAgents`; update `usage()`.
- Flags: `--session-id`, `--epic-id`, `--all`, `--project-root` (exactly one of the first three; `--all` needs `--project-root`).
- Returns JSON object: `{"scope":"epic"|"all","epic_id":<id|null>,"agents":[...]}`. For `--session-id`, resolves epic via `SessionActiveEpic`; null epic ⇒ `epic_id:null, agents:[]`.

### Python — `src/endless/agents_cmd.py` (new) + `cli.py`
- `@main.command("agents")` with `--epic` (TASK_ID type) and `--all`.
- Logic module (mirrors epic_cmd.py thin-wrapper style): validate not-both;
  resolve via `_current_endless_session_id` when neither flag; `config.require_db_context()` then shell to the Go verb with `config.go_db_context_args()`; parse JSON; format.
- Errors (Q3): no current session → guidance; scope==epic and epic_id null → guidance.
- Plain-text table: `Background agents under E-NNNN (N):` header, columns ID/SHORT/TASK/STARTED/TITLE; empty-state line.

## Tests
- Go: `internal/sessionquerycmd/list_bg_agents_test.go` — seed temp DB (bg agent under epic, a tmux session, an ended bg agent, a bg agent in another epic), invoke binary, assert JSON for `--epic-id`, `--session-id` (incl. null-epic), `--all`. Mirror `list_live_test.go`.
- Python: `tests/test_agents_cmd.py` — seed sessions+tasks, invoke `endless agents --epic`, assert rows + empty-state + both-flags error. Mirror `test_epic_cmd.py`.
- Per-task: `tests/tasks/e-1621-verify.sh` (sandbox DB; assert_succeeds/assert_refused), modeled on the E-1577 script.

## Follow-up task (filed, Mike spawns)
"Populate sessions.active_epic_id on interactive claim of an epic-descendant
task" — `--cleans-up E-1571`, `--relates-to E-1621`, `--parent E-1537`. Make
`execTaskClaimed` set `active_epic_id = nearestEpicAncestor(taskID)` (clear on
release / non-epic re-claim). Unblocks E-1621's interactive auto-resolve and the
status-line epic prefix. **E-1621 is blocked-by this task.**

## Verification
- `just build` then `just test` (Python) and `go test ./internal/...` (Go) green.
- `tests/tasks/e-1621-verify.sh` green against a sandbox DB.
- `--epic`/`--all` work immediately; interactive auto-resolve lights up once the
  gap-fill task lands.

payload.title: Populate sessions.active_epic_id on interactive claim of an epic-descendant task
payload.description: execTaskClaimed (internal/events/executor.go) sets only active_task_id on claim, never active_epic_id; E-1571 deferred this coordinator-side write to the claim flow but it was never implemented, so interactive sessions always have active_epic_id NULL. That blocks the E-1571 status-line [E-EEEE:E-CCCC] prefix and the endless agents (E-1621) interactive auto-resolve. Fix: on claim set active_epic_id = nearestEpicAncestor(taskID) (NULL when no epic ancestor); on release clear it too.
payload.text:
# Populate sessions.active_epic_id on interactive task claim

## Context
E-1571 added `sessions.active_epic_id` and specified that a coordinator
(interactive/fg) session working under an epic should carry the epic's id in
`active_epic_id`, with `active_task_id` tracking the viewed child. It explicitly
deferred the *write* for interactive sessions out of its own scope ("part of
E-1570 and the day-to-day `endless task claim` flow"). That write was never
implemented.

Today `active_epic_id` is set **only** for background-agent rows
(`RecordBgAgentSession`, internal/monitor/session.go:341). The claim handler
`execTaskClaimed` (internal/events/executor.go:752) sets only `active_task_id`.
Consequences:
- The E-1571 status-line `[E-EEEE:E-CCCC]` / `[E-EEEE]` epic prefix can never
  render for interactive sessions (it branches on `active_epic_id`, always NULL).
- `endless agents` (E-1621) auto-resolves the active epic from the caller's
  `active_epic_id`, so the interactive auto path always errors until this lands.

## Change
In `execTaskClaimed` (internal/events/executor.go), when updating the session on
claim, also set `active_epic_id = nearestEpicAncestor(taskID)`:
- task has an epic ancestor → that epic id (an epic claimed directly resolves to
  itself, per `nearestEpicAncestor` depth-0 inclusion).
- no epic ancestor → NULL (clears any stale value from a prior claim).

`nearestEpicAncestor` (internal/monitor/session.go:411) is currently unexported
in `monitor`. Either export it (e.g. `NearestEpicAncestor`) for the events
package, or inline the same recursive-CTE resolution in the executor's UPDATE as
a correlated subquery:

```sql
UPDATE sessions
   SET active_task_id = ?,
       active_epic_id = (
         WITH RECURSIVE ancestry(id, parent_id, type_id, depth) AS (
           SELECT id, parent_id, type_id, 0 FROM tasks WHERE id = ?
           UNION ALL
           SELECT t.id, t.parent_id, t.type_id, a.depth+1
             FROM tasks t JOIN ancestry a ON t.id = a.parent_id
         )
         SELECT a.id FROM ancestry a
           JOIN task_types tt ON tt.id = a.type_id
          WHERE tt.slug = 'epic'
          ORDER BY a.depth LIMIT 1
       )
 WHERE id = ?
```

The single-statement subquery avoids exporting the helper and keeps the write
atomic. Pick whichever fits the executor's existing style (it uses raw SQL via
`db.Exec`).

Also handle release: `execTaskReleased` (executor.go:797) NULLs `active_task_id`;
it should NULL `active_epic_id` too, so a released coordinator session doesn't
keep a stale epic.

## Tests
- Extend `internal/events` tests: claim a child of an epic → row has
  `active_epic_id` = epic; claim a standalone task → NULL; claim the epic
  directly → its own id; release → both NULL.
- Confirm the status-line prefix now renders for an interactive session (an
  existing `tmux_lookup`/`status_line` test seeded with `active_epic_id`).

## Verification
- `go test ./internal/events/... ./internal/monitor/...` green.
- Manually: `endless task claim <epic-child>` then check the session row's
  `active_epic_id`; `endless agents` (E-1621) interactive auto-resolve now works.

## Relations
- Cleans up E-1571 (implements its deferred coordinator-write design).
- Relates to E-1621 (which is blocked-by this task).

payload.title: Add a just verify self_dev recipe wrapping endless verify for Endless's own task suites
payload.description: Dev-only Justfile recipe 'just verify [task-id]' (self_dev counterpart to just test) that runs a task's verification while developing Endless by invoking the PRODUCT verb endless verify against Endless's own .endless/tasks suites, with worktree-built binary + sandbox routing. Defaults to the worktree's bound task when no id is given. Per just-is-dev-only, the recipe holds NO verification logic; it only wraps the product runner.
payload.text:
# Plan — `just verify` self_dev recipe

A dev-only Justfile recipe to run a task's verification while developing Endless itself —
the self_dev counterpart to `just test`.

## Behavior

`just verify [task-id]` — invokes the PRODUCT verb `endless verify` against Endless's own
`.endless/tasks/` suites. With no id, defaults to the worktree's bound task (same resolution
other endless commands use). Runs with the worktree-built binary + sandbox routing so the
candidate code is exercised, not the global install.

## Constraint (just-is-dev-only)

The recipe contains NO verification logic — it only wraps `endless verify` (and sets up the
self_dev context). All verification behavior lives in the product runner (E-1603). If the recipe
starts wanting logic, that logic belongs in `endless verify`, not the Justfile.

## Deps

- Blocked by E-1603 (the product `endless verify` runner it wraps).
- Pairs with E-1605 (first dogfood suite) — `just verify` is how that suite gets run in dev.

## Verify

- `just verify E-NNNN` runs that task's checks via the worktree binary against the sandbox and
  reports the merged result.
- `just verify` with no arg resolves the worktree's task and does the same.
- The recipe is a thin wrapper (grep confirms no assertion/normalization logic in the Justfile).

Worktree fully merged (no unmerged commits). E2E proven by production data: real-DB session_tasks now has 478 rows from 2026-05-16T21:41 (minutes after the fix) through 2026-06-23T21:34, where there were zero before. Task was stuck in_progress only because its session was reaped in a tmux crash; never advanced. No code work needed.

payload.title: Fix worktree land writing task.landed to the sandbox DB when run from a self-dev session
payload.description: When `endless worktree land` runs from a shell whose XDG_CONFIG_HOME points at a self-dev sandbox (e.g. a Claude session spawned inside a worktree), its internal `endless-go event emit` for task.landed inherits that routing and writes to the sandbox DB, where the landed task doesn't exist — so the task_landings insert fails the task_id FK (seen landing E-1542: FK 787) and the land can't record. The landing emit should pin to the real/main DB unconditionally, as hook/channel/tmux do via PinMainDB (E-1450/E-1429). E-1474 made this re-runnable but didn't fix the root cause; workaround is to run land with XDG_CONFIG_HOME set to the real config dir.

fields.description: When `just land` / `endless worktree land` runs from a shell whose XDG_CONFIG_HOME points at a self-dev sandbox (e.g. a Claude session spawned inside a worktree), the land's endless calls inherit that routing and target the SANDBOX DB — but landing is inherently a real/main operation and the landed task lives only in the real DB. The first hard failure is the task.landed emit (task_landings task_id FK 787, seen landing E-1542), but schema-apply/backup are mis-targeted too. Landing should pin the real/main DB regardless of caller routing, like hook/channel/tmux do via PinMainDB (E-1450/E-1429). E-1474 made the emit re-runnable but didn't fix the root cause. Workaround: run land with XDG_CONFIG_HOME set to the real config dir.

fields.text:
# E-1532 — scope notes (folded in 2026-06-23)

This task still needs full planning. The notes below are requirements gathered
while diagnosing an E-1621 status-drift incident; fold them into the eventual plan.

## Drop `blocked` from the valid status set

`blocked` should NOT be a seeded value in the new `task_statuses` table — remove it
as a stored status entirely.

Why:
- "Blocked" is already **derived**: the `blocked_by` relation (`task_deps`, which
  supports `source_type` task / decision / project) plus the blocker's status tells
  you whether a dependent is still held (per `endless guide tasks` blocking
  semantics). A stored `status='blocked'` duplicates that and goes **stale** the
  moment the blocker resolves — nothing recomputes it (E-1621 hit exactly this).
- The status field carries **no "why."** Of the 9 current `blocked` rows, 8 have a
  `blocked_by` relation that names the blocker; row **627** has none, so its reason
  is unrecoverable — proof the "why" belongs in the relation, not the status.
- The "what status to restore on unblock?" problem **dissolves** if `blocked` is
  never stored: the task keeps its real work-state (`ready`/`in_progress`/`verify`)
  throughout, and the relation independently conveys the dependency.
- History: `blocked`-as-status predates blocking links — 8 of the 9 rows are from
  the project's first week (2026-04-14..04-19); `1515` (05-29) and E-1621 (06-22)
  are later regressions of the same footgun.

## Reconcile-or-relink the 9 existing `blocked` rows

Once the FK rejects `blocked`, these must be migrated first (one-time ledger
hygiene, not shipped migration code). State captured 2026-06-23:

| Row(s) | active blocker relation | action |
|---|---|---|
| 496, 520 | blocked_by 815 [needs_plan] | reset to real work-state; relation stays |
| 581, 624, 634, 721 | blocked_by 808 [verify] | reset to real work-state; relation stays |
| 616 | blocked_by 811 [needs_plan] | reset to real work-state; relation stays |
| 1515 | blocked_by 1479 [ready] | reset to real work-state; relation stays |
| 627 | **none** | planning-time decision: reconstruct a blocker to link, or set its real status |

"Real work-state": recover the pre-`blocked` status from the event ledger if
present; otherwise set by inspection (`ready` if it has a plan, else `needs_plan`).
627 ("Track stuck-agent detection…") is explicitly left for this task's planning.

## Collapse the duplicated status whitelist

Status is currently validated only in Python, and the whitelist is duplicated:
`cli.py:121 TASK_STATUSES` and the inline tuple in `task_cmd.py:3267`. The DB itself
accepts any string (no values table — the gap this task closes). Make validation
read the seeded `task_statuses` values, so there is one source of truth instead of
three (table + two Python lists).

## Coordinate with E-1252

E-1252 proposes renaming statuses (`needs_plan`/`in_progress` → pending/…); the
values-table seed is the natural place to land any rename. Sequence the two so the
seed reflects the final names.

# E-1628 — Fix worktree land mis-targeting the sandbox DB from a self-dev session

## Context
Discovered while landing E-1542 from a Claude session spawned inside a self-dev
worktree: `just land` failed at the `task.landed` step with a `task_landings`
FK error (787), and only succeeded once `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` was forced to the real
config dir. The landing is a real/main operation, but it was targeting the
per-worktree sandbox DB.

## Root cause (verified 2026-06-23)
The `just land` recipe runs three separate `endless` processes — `endless db
backup`, `endless db apply-change <f>` (per added change file), and `endless
worktree land <id>` (Justfile `land` recipe, ~lines 208-220). None pass `--db`,
so each inherits the invoking shell's `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`. A Claude session spawned
inside a self-dev worktree has that XDG pointed at the sandbox
(`~/.cache/endless/sandboxes/e-NNN`, injected by the worktree's
`.claude/settings.json`), so all three target the **sandbox** DB.

But landing is inherently a real/main operation: the landed task lives only in
the real DB, schema changes belong to the real DB, and the merge is into `main`.
Hard failure: the `task.landed` emit's `task_landings` insert violates the
`task_id` FK because the task isn't in the sandbox; schema-apply also silently
records its `_schema_version` marker in the sandbox. Confirmed fix signal: with
`XDG_CONFIG_HOME=$HOME/.config` the land succeeds end-to-end.

## Fix
Make the land-flow infrastructure commands default to the **real/main DB** when
no explicit `--db` was given — they are "always-main" operations, the Python
analogue of the Go-side `PinMainDB` for hook/channel/tmux (E-1450/E-1429).

At each command's entry, if `config.RESOLVED_CONFIG_DIR is None`, call
`config.apply_db_choice("main")` (it pins `config.main_config_dir()` via
`set_db_context`, so Python reads use the real `DB_PATH` **and**
`go_db_context_args()` then threads `--config-dir <real>` to every downstream
`endless-go` shellout). An explicit `--db main|sandbox` is honored — only force
when unset.

Command entry points to pin:
- `endless worktree land` (`src/endless/cli.py:2160` → `land_worktree` in `worktree_cmd.py`)
- `endless db apply-change`
- `endless db backup`

Putting the default in the commands (not the `just land` recipe) keeps both
`just land` and direct `endless worktree land` invocations correct, so the
behavior ships in the product, not the dev wrapper (per "just is dev-only" /
"ship behavior in product, not machine config").

**Alternative considered & rejected:** have the `just land` recipe pass `--db
main` to its three `endless` calls. Smaller, but leaves direct `endless worktree
land` / `endless db apply-change` invocations exposed and pushes load-bearing
behavior into the dev wrapper instead of the command.

## Out of scope / notes
- E-1474 already made the post-ff-merge `task.landed` emit re-runnable (symptom
  tolerance); this fixes the root cause so the emit doesn't fail to begin with.
- The session resolver returned a sandbox session id (`2`) rather than the
  real-DB id (`722`) from the main checkout. Once the land pins main, session
  resolution also runs against the real DB; confirm during implementation and
  file separately if it persists.

## Tests
- Python unit: invoke `land_worktree` (and the `db apply-change` / `db backup`
  entries) with `RESOLVED_CONFIG_DIR=None` and a sandbox-pointing XDG; assert
  each pins `main_config_dir()` (RESOLVED_CONFIG_DIR becomes the real dir;
  `go_db_context_args()` threads `--config-dir <real>`). Assert an explicit
  `--db sandbox` is NOT overridden.
- Per-task verify script `tests/tasks/e-1628-verify.sh`: from a sandbox-routed
  env (XDG=sandbox, no `--db`), land a throwaway real-DB task and assert the
  `task_landings` row is written to the **real** DB and the FK passes —
  reproducing E-1542's failure on baseline and confirming the fix.

## Critical files
- `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py` (`land_worktree` entry)
- `src/endless/cli.py` (`worktree_land`, `db apply-change`, `db backup` registrations)
- `src/endless/config.py` (reuse `apply_db_choice` / `main_config_dir` / `set_db_context`; no change expected)

## Verification
From a Claude session inside a self-dev worktree (XDG=sandbox), run `just land
E-NNN` with **no** XDG override: the landing records to the real DB, the
`task_landings` FK passes, and `_schema_version` markers land in the real DB.

# E-1532 — Formalize task phases & statuses via values tables — PLAN

Decisions locked 2026-06-23. **Blocked by E-1252** (status renames): the seed must
use E-1252's final names, so that rename decision lands first. Deciding E-1252 is
the real gate to spawning this task.

## Locked decisions
1. **One task.** Phases + statuses values tables, dropping `blocked`, the 9-row
   cleanup, and collapsing the duplicated validation — all here, not split. The
   durable fix is the table; a half-fix now would just be reworked.
2. **Blocked by E-1252.** Final status slugs come from the rename decision; the
   seed reflects its outcome.
3. **Drop `blocked` as a stored status** — not seeded. Blocking is derived from the
   `blocked_by` relation + blocker status (`endless guide tasks`); a stored value
   only duplicates it and goes stale (it bit E-1621) and carries no "why" (row 627
   proved the reason is unrecoverable without a relation). Removing it also
   dissolves the "what status on unblock?" problem — the real work-state is never
   overwritten.
4. **Enforcement = app-level validation reading the values tables**, NOT a DB FK on
   `tasks`. A true FK on the existing `tasks.status`/`.phase` TEXT columns needs a
   full `tasks` table rebuild (SQLite can't ALTER-add a constraint), and `tasks(id)`
   has inbound FKs (`sessions.active_task_id`, `sessions.active_epic_id`,
   `task_deps`, …) that make the rebuild risky for no functional gain. The values
   table still delivers the cited benefit — add/remove a value = INSERT/DELETE, not
   a migration — and becomes the single source the validators read. (DB-level FK
   hardening is a possible later follow-up; see "Out of scope".) Diverges from the
   original "FK columns on tasks" wording — confirmed tradeoff, not an oversight.
5. **Phase values** = the in-use five: `urgent, now, next, later, maybe`.
6. **Status values** = current set minus `blocked`, with E-1252 renames applied:
   (`needs_plan`→?, `in_progress`→?), `ready, verify, confirmed, assumed, completed,
   revisit, declined, obsolete`.
7. **FK-migration mechanics** (for whatever schema change does land): a `.go`
   change-file run with `foreign_keys` OFF, mirroring E-1571's
   `active_epic_id`/`kind_id` change; `schema.sql` declares the post-state for
   fresh/sandbox DBs.

## Steps
1. **schema.sql** — add `task_phases(id, slug UNIQUE NOT NULL, label NOT NULL)` and
   `task_statuses(id, slug UNIQUE NOT NULL, label NOT NULL)`, each with idempotent
   `INSERT OR IGNORE` seeds (phases above; statuses = final post-E-1252 set, no
   `blocked`).
2. **Collapse validation to one source** — replace `cli.py:121 TASK_STATUSES` and
   the inline tuple in `task_cmd.py:3267` (and the phase choices) with a lookup of
   the seeded `task_statuses` / `task_phases` slugs. `click.Choice`/`MultiChoice`
   build from that single source.
3. **Pre-cleanup the 9 `blocked` rows** (re-verify the set at implementation time):
   - 627 → `obsolete` (superseded by E-891).
   - 496, 520, 581, 616, 624, 634, 721, 1515 → `ready` if the row has non-empty
     text, else `needs_plan`; leave their `blocked_by` relations intact.
   One-time ledger hygiene (not shipped migration code); do it before removing
   `blocked` from the validator so nothing reads an invalid value.
4. **Reject `blocked` going forward** — it is simply absent from the source the
   validator reads, so any `--status blocked` write fails with the standard
   "Invalid status" error. No separate gate needed.
5. **Tests** — validator rejects an unseeded status (incl. `blocked`) and accepts
   each seeded one; same for phases; the cleanup leaves zero `blocked` rows.

## Out of scope
- A DB-level FK on `tasks.status`/`.phase` (would need the `tasks` table rebuild
  above) — possible later hardening, not v1.
- The rename strings themselves — owned by E-1252 (this task's blocker).

payload.title: Fix duplicate session rows minted per Claude launch when TMUX_PANE is empty
payload.description: Tasks accrue many sessions rows because the dedup key is session_id (Claude UUID), not active_task_id, and the only dedup path (collision-invalidation by tmux pane) is inert when TMUX_PANE is empty. Each launch INSERTs a fresh-UUID ghost row. Add an active_task_id-scoped fallback dedup for the empty-pane case.

fields.analysis:
Researched 2026-06-24 against the 744-row sessions table.

ROOT CAUSE: dedup key is sessions.session_id (Claude UUID), not active_task_id. Every Claude launch (resume / respawn / aborted spawn / /clear) has a new UUID, so SessionStart's upsert finds no conflict and INSERTs a new row via TouchSession (internal/monitor/session.go:236-295, called from internal/hookcmd/claude.go:161) and InitSession (session.go:104-121). The intended dedup -- collision-invalidation by process/tmux-pane (session.go:280-292) -- is INERT because TMUX_PANE is empty in the hook env: process is NULL on 728/744 rows and COALESCE(NULLIF(excluded.process,'')) refuses to store an empty pane, so there is no pane to collide on.

EVIDENCE: Pattern-A ghost rows (ended, NULL process, no session_statuses, <10s) = 298 DB-wide under the loose filter; 37 under the strict prune criteria (kind_id=1, not-newest-per-task). Examples: task 894 = three 4-9s rows; task 1362 = one real row (id 490) + five sub-10s ghosts.

DISPROVEN: bg-agent double-insert -- decorate-before-touch ordering at claude.go:154 is correct (DecorateBgSession UPDATEs by short_id before TouchSession); all sampled target rows are kind_id=1.

FIX: add an active_task_id-scoped fallback dedup for the empty-pane case so SessionStart folds into / reuses the most-recent non-ended row for the same task instead of inserting a fresh-UUID row.

Other refs: session.go:38-60 BindSessionToTask, :341-375 RecordBgAgentSession, :447-465 DecorateBgSession; claude.go:154-180 decorate->touch->init ordering, :296-298 EndSession (SessionEnd, rarely fires).

payload.title: Remove existing ghost session rows via the event-authoritative layer (not a raw SQL DELETE)
payload.description: Prune the accumulated sub-10s ghost sessions (37 under strict criteria: ended, kind_id=1, <10s, no session_statuses, not newest-per-task; ~298 under loose filter). Must NOT be a raw endless sql --write DELETE: sessions(id) is FK-referenced by 7 tables (session_messages, focuses, task_landings, decisions, session_gates, project_next_*), and per E-807/E-808 the event log is authoritative so a projection rebuild would resurrect deleted rows. Prune at the ledger/compensating-event layer and check FK refs first.

payload.title: Improve session end so rows close on idle-timeout instead of reaper mass-end sweeps
payload.description: Pattern-B: SessionEnd/EndSession (claude.go:296-298) rarely fires (harness is killed, not cleanly exited), so sessions linger state='working' until a later reaper / collision sweep marks a batch 'ended' at one shared instant (e.g. 6 rows all ending 2026-05-27T02:13:07). Effect: last_activity is bumped far past real life, durations span days, and duration becomes useless as a 'real session' signal. End sessions based on last_activity + idle-timeout (heartbeat staleness) rather than wall-clock sweep time, so end timestamps and durations reflect actual session life.

payload.title: Explore guarding against alt-worktree proliferation (one worktree per task invariant)
payload.description: Today a task can spawn additional worktrees at .endless/worktrees/e-<id>-<slug>/. Mike wants to guard toward one-worktree-per-task and instead model legitimate multi-checkout needs (A/B implementations, run-while-edit, bisect/snapshot-compare) as CHILD TASKS of the core task, each with its own plan, plus a compare/pick task. Explore whether to discourage/forbid alt worktrees, what breaks if we do, and how respawn (which follows session->worktree binding) interacts. Policy/research task -- outcome is a recommendation.

payload.title: Build respawn: reopen a resolved task or session back into its worktree
payload.description: Reopen a resolved task/session into its (rebuilt-if-reaped) worktree+sandbox and hand off interrogatively. Driven by discoverability: respawn is an alias surface over a revised spawn --reopen. Liveness-guarded (refuse if a session is already live; navigate to it instead). See children for the split.

payload.title: Revise task spawn --reopen: ensure/rebuild worktree+sandbox, liveness guard, inherit-session
payload.description: Change bare --reopen (currently suboptimal) to: (1) liveness-guard -- if a session is already live for the task, do NOT spawn; foreground -> tmux switch-client to its pane, bg -> point at task attach; (2) ensure/rebuild the worktree -- reuse if present, else fresh branch off main (reaped-by-land/drop both fine), and recreate the self-dev sandbox DB; (3) inherit the most-applicable prior ended session by default (live-first, else newest with dur>=10s OR populated process/transcript_path; skip sub-10s ghosts; never pick by max duration -- Pattern B poisons it); --new-session opts into a fresh session row + fresh worktree.

payload.title: Add task respawn alias and session respawn <id> | --task E-NNN entry points
payload.description: task respawn <id> = alias for task spawn <id> --reopen (pure discoverability). session respawn <id> resolves session -> (task, that session's worktree) and runs the reopen core anchored via --worktree; session respawn --task E-NNN resolves to the most-applicable session for the task (same live-first / skip-ghost rule). Both refuse when a session is already live for the task (navigate to it). Foreground + --bg parity with spawn.

payload.title: Add flat type-agnostic respawn handoff template
payload.description: New handoff/respawn.md.tmpl, single template (NOT per-type -- the four existing templates are initial-spawn instructions and would fight a reopened preamble). It: (1) leads with /cd <abs worktree path>; (2) states the restore case (reused vs rebuilt-off-main) and carries prior outcome + last session_statuses snapshot as read-only context; (3) instructs: this was previously worked/landed -- ASK the user their goal for reopening before doing anything, then run endless guide. Revisit per-type variants later if too blunt.

fields.text:
# E-1532 — Formalize task phases & statuses via FK to values tables — PLAN

Decisions locked 2026-06-23 (rev. 2026-06-24). **Blocked by E-1252** (status
renames): the seed must use E-1252's final names, so that decision lands first.

## Locked decisions
1. **One task.** Phases + statuses values tables, dropping `blocked`, the 9-row
   cleanup, and collapsing the duplicated validation — all here, not split.
2. **Blocked by E-1252.** Final status slugs come from the rename decision; the
   seed (and the one-time row rename) reflect its outcome.
3. **Drop `blocked` as a stored status** — not seeded. Blocking is derived from the
   `blocked_by` relation + blocker status (`endless guide tasks`); a stored value
   only duplicates it and goes stale (it bit E-1621) and carries no "why" (row 627
   proved the reason is unrecoverable without a relation). Removing it also
   dissolves the "what status on unblock?" problem — the real work-state is never
   overwritten.
4. **Enforcement = a real DB-level FK, by SLUG, done now (not deferred).** Keep
   `tasks.status`/`.phase` as TEXT and add `FOREIGN KEY(status) REFERENCES
   task_statuses(slug)` and `FOREIGN KEY(phase) REFERENCES task_phases(slug)`. The
   DB then rejects any invalid value on *every* write path (Go executor, raw SQL,
   Python), and the TEXT column means **no call-site churn** — every reader/writer
   keeps treating status/phase as the same string.
5. **Phase values** = the in-use five: `urgent, now, next, later, maybe`.
6. **Status values** = current set minus `blocked`, with E-1252 renames applied:
   (`needs_plan`→?, `in_progress`→?), `ready, verify, confirmed, assumed, completed,
   revisit, declined, obsolete`.

## Migration mechanics (the FK on existing columns)
SQLite can't ALTER-add a constraint, so the `.go` change-file (run with
`foreign_keys` OFF — the runner's default, per E-1571) does a standard `tasks`
table-rebuild:
1. create+seed `task_phases` / `task_statuses`;
2. run the 9-row cleanup (below) so no row holds a soon-invalid value;
3. `CREATE TABLE tasks_new (… same columns …, FOREIGN KEY(status) REFERENCES
   task_statuses(slug), FOREIGN KEY(phase) REFERENCES task_phases(slug), + existing
   self/other FKs)`;
4. `INSERT INTO tasks_new SELECT * FROM tasks` (ids preserved → 8 inbound-FK tables
   stay valid under FK-off);
5. `DROP TABLE tasks; ALTER TABLE tasks_new RENAME TO tasks`;
6. recreate the `tasks_updated_at` trigger and indexes (`idx_session_tasks_task`,
   `idx_project_next_tasks_task`);
7. `PRAGMA foreign_key_check` before the runner re-enables FKs.
`schema.sql` declares the post-state (the two tables + FK clauses on the `tasks`
CREATE) for fresh/sandbox DBs.

## Steps
1. **schema.sql** — add `task_phases(id, slug UNIQUE NOT NULL, label NOT NULL)` and
   `task_statuses(...)` with idempotent `INSERT OR IGNORE` seeds; add the two FK
   clauses to the `tasks` CREATE.
2. **`.go` change-file** — the rebuild above, against the populated real DB.
3. **Collapse validation to one source** — replace `cli.py:121 TASK_STATUSES` and
   the inline tuple in `task_cmd.py:3267` (and the phase choices) with a lookup of
   the seeded slugs; `click.Choice`/`MultiChoice` build from that single source.
   (The DB FK is the backstop; this keeps the friendly CLI error.)
4. **9-row cleanup** (re-verify the set at implementation time), inside the
   change-file before the rebuild:
   - 627 → `obsolete` (superseded by E-891).
   - 496, 520, 581, 616, 624, 634, 721, 1515 → `ready` if the row has non-empty
     text, else `needs_plan`; leave their `blocked_by` relations intact.
   One-time ledger hygiene (not shipped migration code).
5. **Apply E-1252 renames** — same change-file: `UPDATE tasks SET status=<new>` for
   each renamed slug, seed the table with new slugs, sweep code/docs/guide/handoff
   references off the old strings.
6. **Tests** — DB rejects an unseeded status (incl. `blocked`) and a bad phase;
   accepts each seeded one; cleanup leaves zero `blocked` rows; the rebuild
   preserved inbound FKs (`foreign_key_check` clean).

## Out of scope
- The rename strings themselves — decided by E-1252 (this task's blocker).

# E-1647 — Flat type-agnostic respawn handoff template

**Epic:** E-1644 · **Blocks:** E-1645 · **Layer:** Go template + Python render glue

## Goal
A single `handoff/respawn.md.tmpl` used for *every* reopen, regardless of task type. The
four existing templates (`task`/`bug`/`research`/`epic`) are *initial-spawn* instructions
that drive toward an end-state; a reopened session has no defined end-state yet, so it gets
a distinct, interrogative handoff that says "get into the worktree, then ask the user why
we're here."

## Files
- **New:** `internal/templatecmd/templates/handoff/respawn.md.tmpl` (embedded via the existing
  `//go:embed templates` at `internal/templatecmd/template.go:34-35`).
- **Render glue:** `src/endless/task_cmd.py:3759-3821` (`render_handoff(...)`). Add a path so a
  reopen renders `handoff/respawn` instead of `handoff/<type>`, and passes three NEW vars
  (below). The Go side (`endless-go template render`, `template.go:64-113`) needs no change —
  it just substitutes whatever vars arrive on stdin.

## Template variables
Existing vars available today: `spawned_id, label_prefix, title, spawner_task, return_anchor,
worktree_path, branch, child_count, children_state, bg`.

**Add three** (supplied by the E-1645 reopen path, which sources them):
- `restore_case` — one of `reused` | `rebuilt-off-main` | `recovered-post-drop`.
- `prior_outcome` — the task's `outcome` field (may be empty).
- `last_status_snapshot` — rendered markdown of the latest `session_statuses` row for the
  inherited session (may be empty).

## Template content (requirements, not final prose)
1. **Lead with** `​/cd {{.worktree_path}}` — absolute path, the first instruction.
2. State the **restore case** and that this task/session was **previously resolved**; include
   `{{.prior_outcome}}` and `{{.last_status_snapshot}}` as **read-only context** ("here's what
   the last session did — do not assume it's still your goal").
3. **The pivot:** "This was previously worked on / landed. **Ask the user what their goal is
   for reopening it before doing anything else.** Then run `endless guide` and
   `endless task show {{.spawned_id}} --text`."
4. Keep the lean drive-to-completion rules (don't `worktree land`/`drop` without asking; file
   drive-by work as separate tasks with `--cleans-up`), same as the other templates.

## Notes / gotchas
- Self-dev projects render straight from the embedded source without materializing
  (`template.go:86-94`) — so endless-developing-endless will pick up the new template with no
  on-disk `.tmpl` to commit.
- Per-type respawn variants are explicitly **out of scope** — revisit only if the flat handoff
  proves too blunt (file a follow-up).

## Verification
- `echo '{"spawned_id":1644,"title":"X","worktree_path":"/abs/p","restore_case":"rebuilt-off-main","prior_outcome":"...","last_status_snapshot":"..."}' | endless-go template render handoff/respawn`
  renders cleanly with all three new vars substituted.
- Once E-1645 lands: a real `respawn` pastes this text and the `/cd` line points at the worktree.

# E-1645 — Revise `task spawn --reopen`: liveness guard, ensure/rebuild worktree+sandbox, inherit-session

**Epic:** E-1644 · **Blocked by:** E-1647 · **Layer:** Python (`task_cmd.py`) + a Go/SQL session resolver

This is the linchpin. `--reopen` exists today but is suboptimal; we make it the real reopen core
that `task respawn` and `session respawn` (E-1646) both call.

## Files
- `src/endless/task_cmd.py` — `spawn_plan()` (3902-4219). Reopen validation at 3936-3949; reopen
  execution path ~4053; pre-claim+worktree at 4080-4094; bg/foreground split at 4099-4108.
- Liveness: `_check_task_ownership()` `task_cmd.py:2634-2677`, invoked at 4078.
- Worktree create/reuse: `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py:1055-1121` (idempotent reuse at 1071-1080).
- Sandbox: `internal/sandboxcmd/init.go` (`Provision`, ~63) + `bind.go` (validates sandbox exists,
  68-73), invoked from `worktree_cmd.py:1214-1256` on self-dev worktree creation.
- Handoff render: `render_handoff()` `task_cmd.py:3759-3821` (route to `respawn` per E-1647).

## Behavior changes

### 1. Liveness guard → navigate, don't just error
Today `_check_task_ownership` *raises* when a different live session owns the task. For reopen,
branch on the live session's kind instead of a bare error:
- **Foreground (tmux, has `process` pane):** `tmux switch-client -t <pane>` — **`switch-client`,
  not `select-window`** (works across tmux sessions/clients). Then exit **without spawning**.
- **Background (`kind_id=2`):** print `attach with: endless task attach E-<id>` and exit.
- **No live session:** proceed with reopen.
`--new-session` does not bypass this — a live owner always wins (you wanted *back into* it).

### 2. Ensure / rebuild worktree + sandbox
- **Worktree present** (not reaped): reuse as-is (`worktree_cmd.py:1071-1080` already idempotent).
- **Reaped by land/drop:** create a fresh branch **off `main`** (`create_task_worktree` does
  `git worktree add -b`). Branch-name collision policy: if `task/<id>-<slug>` still exists (drop
  that didn't delete it), reuse it; if it was merged+deleted by land, a fresh branch off main
  already contains the work. Decide + document the suffix rule (e.g. reuse, else `-respawn`).
- **Sandbox:** if the self-dev sandbox under `~/.cache/endless/sandboxes/...` was reaped,
  re-`Provision` + re-`bind` (idempotent). `bind` currently *requires* an existing sandbox, so
  ensure init runs first.

### 3. Inherit the most-applicable prior session (default)
Resolve the session whose worktree + context we reopen into. **Live-first is handled by the
guard above**, so here we pick among *ended* rows:

```sql
SELECT id, session_id, process, transcript_path, started_at, last_activity, state,
       (julianday(last_activity)-julianday(started_at))*86400 AS dur
FROM sessions
WHERE active_task_id = :task AND state = 'ended'
ORDER BY (process IS NOT NULL OR transcript_path IS NOT NULL OR dur >= 10) DESC,
         started_at DESC
LIMIT 1;
```
- **Skip sub-10s ghosts; never order by `dur` descending** (Pattern B makes a stale real row
  span days). This defends against E-1640 ghosts *regardless of whether E-1640 has landed*.
- Use the inherited session's worktree (may be an alt `e-<id>-<slug>`) and its `outcome` +
  latest `session_statuses` row to populate the E-1647 handoff vars.
- `--new-session`: skip inheritance → fresh session row + fresh worktree off main.

### 4. Render the flat respawn handoff
Call `render_handoff(..., task_type="respawn", restore_case=..., prior_outcome=...,
last_status_snapshot=...)`. Keep foreground + `--bg` parity (reuse the existing split).

## Verification
- Reopen a `confirmed` task whose worktree was landed-away → fresh worktree off main + sandbox +
  respawn handoff whose `/cd` points at it.
- Reopen a task with a **live** foreground session → `switch-client` to its pane, **no** spawn.
- Reopen with `--new-session` → brand-new session row + fresh worktree.
- Seed a sub-10s ghost on the task, then reopen → resolver picks the real session, not the ghost.

## Risks / open decisions
- Branch-name collision policy on reaped-but-branch-exists (drop case).
- `--bg` reopen has no interactive "ask the user the goal" step — confirm the respawn handoff
  still reads sensibly for a bg agent (it should ask in its first turn).

# E-1646 — `task respawn` alias + `session respawn <id> | --task E-NNN` entry points

**Epic:** E-1644 · **Blocked by:** E-1645 · **Layer:** Python CLI

Pure surface work on top of the E-1645 reopen core. The whole reason this feature exists is
**discoverability** — `respawn` was reached for and `--help` showed nothing.

## Files
- `src/endless/cli.py`: add `endless task respawn` near the spawn definition (1559-1598); add
  `endless session respawn` to the session group (after the last session subcommand, ~line 926,
  before the `session status` subgroup at 928).
- `src/endless/task_cmd.py`: thin `task_respawn()` wrapper.
- `src/endless/session_cmd.py`: `session_respawn()` resolver + dispatch.

## Commands

### `endless task respawn <id>`
Exact alias for `endless task spawn <id> --reopen`. Pass through `--bg`, `--worktree`,
`--new-session`, `--force`. No new logic — just forwards into `spawn_plan(..., reopen=True)`.

### `endless session respawn <session-id>`
1. Resolve the session row → its `active_task_id` and its **worktree path** (from the session's
   recorded cwd / `worktree.json`, or convention `WorktreePathForTask`; may be an alt
   `e-<id>-<slug>`, since this verb is explicitly session-anchored).
2. Call the E-1645 reopen core anchored via `--worktree <that path>`.
3. Same liveness guard: if a session is already live for that task, navigate to it
   (`switch-client` / `task attach`) instead of spawning.

### `endless session respawn --task E-NNN`
Resolve the **most-applicable** session for the task using the E-1645 resolver (live-first;
else newest ended with `dur>=10s` OR populated `process`/`transcript_path`; **skip sub-10s
ghosts**), then proceed as `session respawn <id>`. Document that ghosts are skipped so the
behavior is predictable even before E-1640 lands.

`<session-id>` and `--task` are mutually exclusive; exactly one required.

## Verification
- `endless task respawn E-NNN` is byte-for-byte equivalent in effect to `spawn E-NNN --reopen`.
- `endless session respawn <id>` lands in *that* session's worktree (verify with an alt worktree).
- `endless session respawn --task E-NNN` on a task with several sessions picks the real one,
  ignoring a seeded sub-10s ghost.
- Help text: `endless task --help` and `endless session --help` now list `respawn`.

## Notes
- Keep these wrappers dumb: all real behavior (guard, ensure-worktree, resolver, handoff) lives
  in E-1645's core so the two surfaces can't drift.

# E-1640 — Fix duplicate session rows minted per Claude launch when TMUX_PANE is empty

**Independent (parent of E-1641).** Full evidence is in this task's `analysis` field. · **Layer:** Go

## Root cause (recap)
Dedup key is `sessions.session_id` (the Claude UUID), not `active_task_id`. Every launch
(resume / respawn / aborted spawn / `/clear`) gets a new UUID, so SessionStart's upsert finds no
conflict and **INSERTs a new row**. The only intended dedup — collision-invalidation by
`process` (tmux pane), `internal/monitor/session.go:280-292` — is **inert** because `TMUX_PANE`
is empty in the hook env: `process` is NULL on 728/744 rows and `COALESCE(NULLIF(excluded.process,''))`
refuses to store an empty pane, so there's no pane to collide on. Sub-10s "ghost" rows accrue.

## Files
- `internal/monitor/session.go`: `TouchSession` (220-295), `InitSession` (104-121), collision
  invalidation (280-292), `BindSessionToTask` (38-60).
- `internal/hookcmd/claude.go`: SessionStart ordering (154-180), `TouchSession` call (~161).

## Approach — active_task_id-scoped fallback dedup for the empty-pane case
When `process` is empty (so pane-collision can't fire) **and** `active_task_id` is known, fold
into the existing recent non-ended row for that task instead of inserting a fresh-UUID row:

- **Is the task known at SessionStart?** For spawned sessions, yes — `@endless_task_id` is set on
  the tmux window and read by the hook (pre-claim). Confirm the binding order: if
  `BindSessionToTask` runs *after* `TouchSession`, the fallback may need to move/peek the task id
  earlier, or run on the bind step.
- **Fold rule:** if a non-ended row exists for the same `active_task_id` with empty `process`
  and recent `last_activity`, reuse it (update `session_id` + `last_activity`) rather than INSERT.
  Scope tightly: same task, empty process, recent — so two genuinely concurrent sessions on one
  task aren't merged.
- **Ad-hoc (unbound) sessions:** task unknown → can't key on task; they still create a row
  (acceptable — a real session until bound). Consider a secondary identity key (transcript path
  / resume id) to dedup relaunches of the *same logical* session, if cheap.

## Verification
- Repeatedly spawn+abort a session on one task → row count stops climbing (folds to one).
- Re-run the ghost-count query (in `analysis`) over several launches → strictly non-growing.
- A genuinely new, separately-claimed session on the same task is NOT folded into a stale row.

## Risk
Over-folding two real concurrent sessions. Mitigate with the tight scope (empty process + recent
+ same task) and a recency window.

# E-1641 — Remove existing ghost session rows (light prune; FK-safe)

**Child of E-1640.** · **Layer:** SQL + small Python/Go command

## Correction from recon
Earlier worry was that an event-authoritative write path would resurrect deleted rows. **Recon
disproved this:** `internal/events/projector.go:73-111` replays **only task/decision events**;
session/note/message events are skipped (line 108). So `sessions` is **not** event-sourced and a
raw `DELETE` **is durable**. **Re-verify** this (read `projector.go`, confirm no `sessions`
replay) as step 0 before deleting — but expect it to hold.

## FK-safety (the real constraint)
`sessions(id)` is referenced by **7 tables**: `session_messages`, `focuses`, `task_landings`,
`decisions`, `session_gates`, `project_next_events`, `project_next_revisions`. The strict
criteria only excludes `session_statuses`-bearing rows, so before deleting, confirm each
candidate id has **zero** references across all 7. Exclude any that are referenced.

## Candidate set
Strict (safe) criteria — **37 rows**:
```sql
WITH latest AS (
  SELECT active_task_id, MAX(started_at) AS max_started FROM sessions GROUP BY active_task_id
)
SELECT s.id FROM sessions s JOIN latest l ON l.active_task_id = s.active_task_id
WHERE s.state='ended' AND s.session_id IS NOT NULL AND s.kind_id=1
  AND (julianday(s.last_activity)-julianday(s.started_at))*86400 < 10
  AND (SELECT count(*) FROM session_statuses ss WHERE ss.session_id=s.id)=0
  AND s.started_at <> l.max_started;
```
The loose filter (sub-10s + ended + no status) yields **~298** including **NULL-`active_task_id`
ghosts** that the "not newest per task" guard can't cover. **Decide:** ship the strict 37 first,
then evaluate the NULL-task set under a separate guard (e.g. "not the newest ended row per
`process`/UUID"). Don't bulk-delete the 298 blind.

## Approach — prefer a repeatable command over a one-off DELETE
Add `endless session prune [--dry-run]` (Python, alongside other `session_cmd.py` verbs):
- `--dry-run` lists exactly which ids match + their FK-reference counts.
- Without it, FK-check each candidate, exclude referenced rows, `DELETE` the rest.
- Repeatable: useful until E-1640 lands and stops new ghosts from minting.
- Run **after** E-1640 so the table doesn't immediately re-accumulate.

## Verification
- `--dry-run` lists the 37 (minus any FK-referenced) and nothing else.
- After prune: ghost-count query → 0 for the strict set; all 7 FK tables intact.
- `endless-go event rebuild-db --dry-run` (or equivalent) does **not** resurrect the deleted rows.

# E-1642 — Close sessions on idle-timeout (there is no reaper today)

**Independent.** · **Layer:** Go (`internal/monitor/session.go`) + a trigger

## Recon correction
There is **NO periodic reaper**. Sessions transition to `ended` only via:
- `EndSession` on the `SessionEnd` hook (`session.go:511-523`) — which **rarely fires** because
  the harness is usually killed, not cleanly exited; and
- collision-invalidation in `TouchSession` (`session.go:280-292`) — **inert** when `process` is
  empty (the norm).

So real, dead sessions linger in `state='working'/'idle'` indefinitely, and the clustered
"mass-end" timestamps seen in the data come from bulk/collision events, **not** a reaper. The fix
is to **add** truthful idle-timeout ending, not to fix a sweep.

## Why it matters
- `duration` is currently a lie — a real session can show a multi-day span — so it can't be used
  as a "this is the real session" signal. E-1645's respawn resolver and the dashboard both want
  reliable `state` + `last_activity`.

## Approach
1. **Add an idle-timeout sweep:** any session in `working`/`idle`/`needs_input` whose
   `last_activity` is older than a configurable window (e.g. 60-90 min; align with the ~1h bg
   idle stop) → mark `ended`, setting the end at **`last_activity`, not `now()`** (don't bump
   the timestamp forward). Note `EndSession` today updates `last_activity` (511-523) — the idle
   path must NOT, so durations stay truthful.
2. **Trigger** (no daemon exists): recommend BOTH
   - piggyback a cheap project-scoped stale-sweep inside `TouchSession` (runs on every hook
     event, so it self-heals during normal activity), and
   - an explicit `endless session sweep [--dry-run]` for manual/cron/tmux-integration use.
3. **Config:** idle window in `.endless/config.json` (via go-cfgstore), with a sane default.

## Risks
- Don't end a session that's legitimately parked on `needs_input` for a long time — consider a
  longer timeout (or exemption) for `needs_input` vs `working`/`idle`.
- The harness resumes on wake (v2.1.142+); don't prematurely end across a machine-sleep gap —
  base the decision on `last_activity` heartbeats, not wall-clock since spawn.

## Verification
- Leave a `working` session idle past the timeout → it flips to `ended` with the **historical**
  `last_activity`, and its computed duration is realistic (not days).
- `endless session sweep --dry-run` lists exactly the stale sessions it would end.

# E-1643 — Explore guarding the one-worktree-per-task invariant

**Independent. Research/policy task — the deliverable is a recommendation in `outcome`, not code.**

## Question
Today a task may spawn additional worktrees at `.endless/worktrees/e-<id>-<slug>/`
(`WorktreePathForTask`, `internal/monitor/worktree.go:15-54`, globs both `e-<id>` and
`e-<id>-*`). Mike wants to lean toward **one worktree per task** and model genuine
multi-checkout needs as **child tasks** instead. Should we discourage / warn / forbid alt
worktrees?

## What to investigate
1. **Where alt worktrees get created/allowed:** `worktree_cmd.py:1055-1121` create path; the
   `e-<id>-*` glob in `worktree.go:15-54`; anywhere that assumes >1 worktree per task.
2. **Legitimate multi-checkout needs and the child-task model that replaces them:**
   - A/B-ing two implementations → two child tasks (each plan describes its approach) + a third
     "compare and pick" child task.
   - Run-while-edit (one checkout running the app/tests, one editing) → child task.
   - Bisect / snapshot-compare → child task.
   Assess whether the child-task model actually covers each case cleanly.
3. **What breaks if alt worktrees are forbidden/discouraged:** tests, the `@endless_task_id`
   binding, `worktree land`/`drop` semantics, dashboard assumptions.
4. **Interaction with respawn (E-1644):** respawn follows the session→worktree binding, so it's
   correct either way; if alt worktrees go away, respawn always targets the primary `e-<id>`.

## Deliverable
Write the recommendation to this task's `outcome`: **forbid / warn-only / leave as-is**, with
rationale, the list of code touch-points, and a migration note. Any actual guard/enforcement
becomes a separate follow-on task, not part of this one.

## Verification
Research task — `outcome` populated with a clear recommendation and evidence. No code change here.

payload.title: Add a human-approval gate before task ready (unapproved status + approve verb + background lockout)
payload.description: Plan-attach auto-promotes needs_plan->ready, so 'ready' means 'agent drafted a plan', not 'Mike approved it' — Mike can't tell reviewed from unreviewed and a background loop can't trust 'ready'. Add the lifecycle: unplanned -> (agent attaches plan) unapproved -> (Mike runs endless task approve) ready. Remove the auto-promote; add an approve verb; forbid kind=background sessions from approve and from picking up non-ready work (system knows session kind). tmux-agent approve stays a convention.
payload.text:
# Add a human-approval gate before task `ready`

## Problem
Attaching a plan via `task update --text` auto-promotes `needs_plan → ready`. So
`ready` means "an agent drafted a plan," not "Mike approved it" — Mike can't tell
reviewed from unreviewed (no DB signal), and a background loop can't safely pick
`ready` work. (Hit live on E-1532 and E-1621.)

## Target lifecycle
```
unplanned → (agent attaches plan) unapproved → (Mike: endless task approve) ready
          → underway → unverified → confirmed
```
`ready` now provably means human-approved.

## Behavior changes
1. **Remove the auto-promote.** `task update --text` no longer flips status to
   `ready`. A plan-attach on an `unplanned` task lands it at **`unapproved`**.
2. **Add `endless task approve <id>`** — the only `unapproved → ready` transition.
3. **Background lockout (the real gate).** `kind=background` sessions are refused
   `approve`, and refused pickup of any non-`ready` task. The system already knows
   session kind (`sessionkind`: tmux vs background), so this is enforceable.
4. **Background pickup = `ready` only.** Any "pick up actionable work" path for
   background loops selects `status=ready` exclusively.

## Known limitation (accepted)
A tmux-driven agent (a Claude session) running `approve` stays a **convention** —
the system can't distinguish a human from an agent in a tmux pane. Removing the
auto-promote kills the *accidental* skip (the actual bug); the background lockout
draws the hard line at autonomous agents. Anything stricter (e.g. an interactive
TTY confirm) is out of scope unless revisited.

## Depends on
The `unapproved` status existing — seeded by E-1532 (values table). Blocked by it.

## Out of scope
- The status renames / values table themselves (E-1252 / E-1532).

payload.title: Task 'ready' requires explicit human approval; agent plan-attach lands at 'unapproved'
payload.description: Plan-attach by an agent no longer auto-promotes to 'ready'. Lifecycle becomes: unplanned -> (agent attaches plan) unapproved -> (human runs endless task approve) ready -> underway -> unverified -> confirmed. 'ready' now provably means human-approved, so background loops may pick up only 'ready' work. Enforcement gates: (1) remove the auto-promote; (2) forbid kind=background sessions from approve and from picking up non-ready work (session kind is known). A tmux-driven agent running approve stays a convention (the system can't distinguish human from agent in a pane) — accepted. Related calls in the same status-model rework: 'blocked' is dropped as a stored status (blocking is derived from the blocked_by relation), and the vocabulary is renamed (needs_plan->unplanned, in_progress->underway, verify->unverified) with a new 'unapproved' state — see E-1252/E-1532.

fields.title: Rework task-status vocabulary: rename needs_plan/in_progress/verify, add unapproved, drop blocked
fields.text:
# E-1252 — Rework task-status vocabulary — PLAN

Decided 2026-06-24 (see ED-1513). Implements the rename half of the status-model
rework; the values table + FK + `blocked` drop are E-1532, the approval-gate
behavior is E-1648.

## Decided vocabulary
| old | new | note |
|---|---|---|
| `needs_plan` | **`unplanned`** | no plan yet; no underscore |
| (new) | **`unapproved`** | plan attached, awaiting Mike's approval (the gate's review state) |
| `in_progress` | **`underway`** | |
| `verify` | **`unverified`** | done, awaiting verification |
| `blocked` | — | dropped (E-1532); blocking is the `blocked_by` relation |
Unchanged: `ready` (now = approved), `confirmed`, `assumed`, `completed`,
`revisit`, `declined`, `obsolete`. The three "un-" states (`unplanned` /
`unapproved` / `unverified`) are the gate-awaiting states.

## Implementation
The data + seed changes ride **E-1532's `.go` change-file** (one rebuild):
`UPDATE tasks SET status=<new>` per renamed slug; seed `task_statuses` with the
new slugs. This task owns the **reference sweep** off the old strings.

## Reference sweep (every occurrence of `needs_plan`/`in_progress`/`verify`)
- **Python**: `cli.py` `TASK_STATUSES` + `click.Choice`/help text; `task_cmd.py`
  status literals and logic (`== 'needs_plan'`, `== 'in_progress'`, `== 'verify'`,
  the `valid` tuple, `task active`/`next`/`show` filters); any other module.
- **Go**: `internal/monitor`, `internal/events`, `internal/tmuxcmd`, web — any
  status string literals.
- **Docs (Mike's call-out)**: `endless guide` (status table, happy path, blocking
  semantics), **CLAUDE.md** (project + global — they reference statuses and the
  "set verify when done" step), **README.md**, **handoff templates**
  (`docs/templates/handoff*` + per-type variants), `agent_help` / guide
  cross-reference.
- **Web UI**: status labels/badges.
- Re-grep at implementation time; the list above is the known surface, not a
  guarantee of completeness.

## Note
Workflow-meaning changes (`ready` = approved, the `approve` step, `unverified`
phrasing in the verify-handoff) are **E-1648's** doc edits — coordinate so the two
sweeps don't clobber the same handoff/guide sections. This task changes the
*names*; E-1648 changes the *workflow prose*.

# Add a human-approval gate before task `ready`

## Problem
Attaching a plan via `task update --text` auto-promotes `needs_plan → ready`. So
`ready` means "an agent drafted a plan," not "Mike approved it" — Mike can't tell
reviewed from unreviewed (no DB signal), and a background loop can't safely pick
`ready` work. (Hit live on E-1532, E-1621, and E-1648 itself.)

## Target lifecycle
```
unplanned → (agent attaches plan) unapproved → (Mike: endless task approve) ready
          → underway → unverified → confirmed
```
`ready` now provably means human-approved.

## Behavior changes
1. **Remove the auto-promote.** `task update --text` no longer flips status to
   `ready`. A plan-attach on an `unplanned` task lands it at **`unapproved`**.
2. **Add `endless task approve <id>`** — the only `unapproved → ready` transition.
3. **Background lockout (the real gate).** `kind=background` sessions are refused
   `approve`, and refused pickup of any non-`ready` task. The system already knows
   session kind (`sessionkind`: tmux vs background), so this is enforceable.
4. **Background pickup = `ready` only.** Any "pick up actionable work" path for
   background loops selects `status=ready` exclusively.

## Docs / workflow-prose sweep (Mike's call-out — the gate half)
The *names* are E-1252's sweep; this task owns the *workflow* prose wherever it
describes how a task reaches done:
- **`endless guide`** — the "happy path" (plan-attach no longer means ready; add
  the `approve` step), the status table's `ready`/`unapproved` semantics, and the
  verify-handoff wording (`verify`→`unverified`).
- **CLAUDE.md** (project + global) — the "set status to verify when done" / "don't
  mark confirmed" guidance grows a "ready requires approval; agents don't approve"
  rule.
- **handoff templates** (`docs/templates/handoff*` + per-type variants) — the
  completion step ("set `verify`") and any "ready" language.
- **README.md** — any lifecycle description.

## Known limitation (accepted)
A tmux-driven agent (a Claude session) running `approve` stays a **convention** —
the system can't distinguish a human from an agent in a tmux pane. Removing the
auto-promote kills the *accidental* skip (the actual bug); the background lockout
draws the hard line at autonomous agents. Anything stricter (e.g. an interactive
TTY confirm) is out of scope unless revisited.

## Depends on
The `unapproved` status existing — seeded by E-1532 (values table). Blocked by it.

# E-1640 — Fix duplicate session rows minted per Claude launch when TMUX_PANE is empty

**Independent (parent of E-1641).** Full evidence in this task's `analysis` field. · **Layer:** Go

## Root cause (recap)
Dedup key is `sessions.session_id` (the Claude UUID), not `active_task_id`. Every launch gets a
new UUID, so SessionStart's upsert finds no conflict and **INSERTs a new row** via `TouchSession`
(`internal/monitor/session.go:236-295`, from `internal/hookcmd/claude.go:161`) and `InitSession`
(`session.go:104-121`). The only intended dedup — collision-invalidation by `process` (tmux pane),
`session.go:280-292` — is **inert** because `TMUX_PANE` is empty: `process` is NULL on 728/744
rows and `COALESCE(NULLIF(excluded.process,''))` refuses to store an empty pane. Sub-10s ghosts accrue.

## Files
- `internal/monitor/session.go`: `TouchSession` (220-295), `InitSession` (104-121), collision
  invalidation (280-292), `BindSessionToTask` (38-60).
- `internal/hookcmd/claude.go`: SessionStart ordering (154-180), `TouchSession` call (~161).

## Approach — active_task_id-scoped fallback dedup for the empty-pane case
When `process` is empty **and** `active_task_id` is known, fold into the existing recent
non-ended row for that task instead of inserting a fresh-UUID row:
- **Task known at SessionStart?** For spawned sessions yes (`@endless_task_id` window var read by
  the hook). Confirm binding order: if `BindSessionToTask` runs *after* `TouchSession`, move/peek
  the task id earlier or run the fallback on the bind step.
- **Fold rule:** if a non-ended row exists for the same `active_task_id` with empty `process` and
  recent `last_activity`, reuse it (update `session_id` + `last_activity`) rather than INSERT.
  Scope tightly (same task + empty process + recent) so two genuinely concurrent sessions aren't merged.
- **Ad-hoc (unbound) sessions:** task unknown → still create a row (real session until bound).

## Verification — create a per-task verify script
Create `tests/tasks/e-1640-verify.sh` in the `e-1624-verify.sh` shape (bash, `set -u`, the
`section`/`report_*`/`summary` helpers, `cd` repo root, ensure `go.work`, exit 0/1/2). Drive the
REAL code path via Go tests in `internal/monitor` — don't re-implement dedup logic.

The script asserts (named checks):
- `internal/monitor` compiles (`go build ./internal/monitor/`).
- A Go test (`go test -count=1 -run ^TestTouchSession_FoldsEmptyPaneSameTask$ ./internal/monitor/`)
  drives repeated `TouchSession` with empty `process` + the same `active_task_id` and asserts the
  row count stays **1** (folds), not N.
- A second Go test asserts a genuinely separate, freshly-claimed session on the same task is **NOT**
  folded into the stale row.
- `internal/monitor` full suite stays green (`go test -count=1 ./internal/monitor/`).

Re-runnability (sandbox/test DB):
- Tests build their own schema-applied DB and derive ids freshly — never hardcode a UNIQUE
  `session_id`/`short_id`; use fresh per-run values.
- Run the script at least TWICE before declaring it done.

**Verify-handoff — tell the user to verify with (only) this:**
```
esu && ./tests/tasks/e-1640-verify.sh
```

# E-1641 — Remove existing ghost session rows (light prune; FK-safe)

**Child of E-1640.** · **Layer:** SQL + small Python/Go command

## Correction from recon
Earlier worry was that an event-authoritative write path would resurrect deleted rows. **Recon
disproved this:** `internal/events/projector.go:73-111` replays **only task/decision events**;
session/note/message events are skipped (line 108). So `sessions` is **not** event-sourced and a
raw `DELETE` **is durable**. **Re-verify** this (read `projector.go`) as step 0 before deleting.

## FK-safety (the real constraint)
`sessions(id)` is referenced by **7 tables**: `session_messages`, `focuses`, `task_landings`,
`decisions`, `session_gates`, `project_next_events`, `project_next_revisions`. Before deleting,
confirm each candidate id has **zero** references across all 7; exclude any that are referenced.

## Candidate set
Strict (safe) criteria — **37 rows**:
```sql
WITH latest AS (
  SELECT active_task_id, MAX(started_at) AS max_started FROM sessions GROUP BY active_task_id
)
SELECT s.id FROM sessions s JOIN latest l ON l.active_task_id = s.active_task_id
WHERE s.state='ended' AND s.session_id IS NOT NULL AND s.kind_id=1
  AND (julianday(s.last_activity)-julianday(s.started_at))*86400 < 10
  AND (SELECT count(*) FROM session_statuses ss WHERE ss.session_id=s.id)=0
  AND s.started_at <> l.max_started;
```
The loose filter yields **~298** incl. NULL-`active_task_id` ghosts the "not newest per task"
guard can't cover. **Decide:** ship the strict 37 first; evaluate the NULL-task set separately
(guard e.g. "not the newest ended row per `process`/UUID"). Don't bulk-delete 298 blind.

## Approach — a repeatable command, not a one-off DELETE
Add `endless session prune [--dry-run]` (Python, alongside `session_cmd.py` verbs):
- `--dry-run` lists exactly which ids match + their FK-reference counts.
- Without it, FK-check each candidate, exclude referenced rows, `DELETE` the rest.
- Repeatable: useful until E-1640 stops new ghosts. Run **after** E-1640.

## Verification — create a per-task verify script
Create `tests/tasks/e-1641-verify.sh` in the `e-1624-verify.sh` shape (bash, `set -u`, the
`section`/`report_*`/`summary` helpers, `cd` repo root, ensure `go.work`, exit 0/1/2). Drive the
real `endless session prune` CLI against sandbox fixtures.

The script asserts (named checks):
- **Dry-run precision:** seed (fresh ids) N sub-10s ended ghosts + one ≥10s real session on a
  task; `endless session prune --dry-run` lists **exactly** the ghosts (with FK-ref counts) and
  excludes the real session.
- **FK-safety:** add a `session_messages`/`decisions` row referencing one ghost; assert the
  dry-run **excludes** that referenced ghost.
- **Prune effect:** after a real prune, the ghost-count query returns 0 for the seeded set; the
  real session and all 7 FK tables are intact.
- **Not event-sourced:** `endless-go event rebuild-db` (dry-run/temp DB) does **not** resurrect
  the pruned rows.

Re-runnability (sandbox NOT wiped between runs):
- Derive all seeded ids/`session_id`/`short_id` from freshly-allocated ids each run — never a
  fixed UNIQUE value (the prune is idempotent, but the *seeding* must not collide).
- Capture seeding output; `exit 2` loudly on any setup/seed error (never /dev/null).
- Run the script at least TWICE before declaring it done.

**Verify-handoff — tell the user to verify with (only) this:**
```
esu && ./tests/tasks/e-1641-verify.sh
```

# E-1642 — Close sessions on idle-timeout (there is no reaper today)

**Independent.** · **Layer:** Go (`internal/monitor/session.go`) + a trigger

## Recon correction
There is **NO periodic reaper**. Sessions reach `ended` only via `EndSession` on `SessionEnd`
(`session.go:511-523`, rarely fires — harness is killed, not cleanly exited) or collision-
invalidation (`session.go:280-292`, inert when `process` is empty). So real dead sessions linger
`working`/`idle` indefinitely; the clustered "mass-end" timestamps come from bulk/collision
events, not a reaper. The fix is to **add** truthful idle-timeout ending.

## Why it matters
`duration` is currently a lie (a real session can span days), so it can't signal "the real
session." E-1645's resolver and the dashboard both want reliable `state` + `last_activity`.

## Approach
1. **Idle-timeout sweep:** any session in `working`/`idle`/`needs_input` whose `last_activity` is
   older than a configurable window (e.g. 60-90 min; align with the ~1h bg idle) → mark `ended`
   with end == **`last_activity`, not `now()`** (don't bump the timestamp). `EndSession` today
   updates `last_activity` (511-523) — the idle path must NOT.
2. **Trigger** (no daemon exists): BOTH (a) a cheap project-scoped stale-sweep piggybacked inside
   `TouchSession` (runs every hook event → self-heals during activity), and (b) an explicit
   `endless session sweep [--dry-run]` for manual/cron/tmux use.
3. **Config:** idle window in `.endless/config.json` (go-cfgstore) with a sane default.

## Risks
- Don't end a session legitimately parked on `needs_input` — longer timeout/exemption for it.
- Harness resumes on wake (v2.1.142+); base the decision on `last_activity` heartbeats, not
  wall-clock since spawn, so a machine-sleep gap doesn't end a live session.

## Verification — create a per-task verify script
Create `tests/tasks/e-1642-verify.sh` in the `e-1624-verify.sh` shape (bash, `set -u`, the
`section`/`report_*`/`summary` helpers, `cd` repo root, ensure `go.work`, exit 0/1/2). Drive the
REAL code path via Go tests in `internal/monitor` plus the `endless session sweep` CLI.

The script asserts (named checks):
- `internal/monitor` compiles.
- A Go test drives a `working`/`idle` session whose `last_activity` is older than the timeout and
  asserts it flips to `ended` with end == the **historical** `last_activity` (NOT `now()`).
- A recent (within-timeout) session is left untouched.
- A `needs_input` session honors its longer leniency (not ended at the short timeout).
- `endless session sweep --dry-run` lists **exactly** the stale sessions it would end (seed via
  fresh ids); `internal/monitor` full suite stays green.

Re-runnability (sandbox NOT wiped between runs):
- Derive seeded ids/`session_id`/`short_id` from freshly-allocated ids each run; set
  `last_activity` relative to a computed "now minus N" each run, never a fixed timestamp.
- Capture seeding output; `exit 2` loudly on any setup/seed error (never /dev/null).
- Run the script at least TWICE before declaring it done.

**Verify-handoff — tell the user to verify with (only) this:**
```
esu && ./tests/tasks/e-1642-verify.sh
```

# E-1643 — Explore guarding the one-worktree-per-task invariant

**Independent. Research/policy task — the deliverable is a recommendation in `outcome`, not code.**

## Question
Today a task may spawn additional worktrees at `.endless/worktrees/e-<id>-<slug>/`
(`WorktreePathForTask`, `internal/monitor/worktree.go:15-54`, globs both `e-<id>` and `e-<id>-*`).
Mike wants to lean toward **one worktree per task** and model genuine multi-checkout needs as
**child tasks**. Should we discourage / warn / forbid alt worktrees?

## What to investigate
1. **Where alt worktrees get created/allowed:** `worktree_cmd.py:1055-1121`; the `e-<id>-*` glob
   in `worktree.go:15-54`; anywhere assuming >1 worktree per task.
2. **Legitimate multi-checkout needs and the child-task model that replaces them:** A/B-ing two
   implementations → two child tasks + a "compare and pick" child; run-while-edit → child task;
   bisect/snapshot-compare → child task. Assess whether child tasks cleanly cover each case.
3. **What breaks if alt worktrees are forbidden/discouraged:** tests, `@endless_task_id` binding,
   `worktree land`/`drop` semantics, dashboard assumptions.
4. **Interaction with respawn (E-1644):** respawn follows the session→worktree binding, so it's
   correct either way; if alt worktrees go away, respawn always targets the primary `e-<id>`.

## Deliverable
Write the recommendation to this task's `outcome`: **forbid / warn-only / leave as-is**, with
rationale, code touch-points, and a migration note. Any actual guard/enforcement becomes a
separate follow-on task.

## Verification — create a per-task verify script
This is a **research task with no code deliverable**, so the verify script is intentionally
minimal — a research outcome is reviewed by *reading* it, not by assertions. Create
`tests/tasks/e-1643-verify.sh` in the `e-1624-verify.sh` shape (bash, `set -u`, the
`section`/`report_*`/`summary` helpers, exit 0/1/2) that asserts only the deliverable exists:
- `endless task show E-1643 --outcome` is **non-empty** and names one of `forbid` / `warn` /
  `leave` (a concrete recommendation was recorded, not an empty hand-off).

If E-1643 instead spawns a follow-on guard/enforcement task, that task carries its own real
verify script; this one just guards against an empty research hand-off. Run it twice to confirm
it's re-runnable.

**Verify-handoff — tell the user to verify with (only) this:**
```
esu && ./tests/tasks/e-1643-verify.sh
```

# E-1645 — Revise `task spawn --reopen`: liveness guard, ensure/rebuild worktree+sandbox, inherit-session

**Epic:** E-1644 · **Blocked by:** E-1647 · **Layer:** Python (`task_cmd.py`) + a Go/SQL session resolver

The linchpin: `--reopen` exists but is suboptimal; we make it the real reopen core that
`task respawn` and `session respawn` (E-1646) both call.

## Files
- `src/endless/task_cmd.py` — `spawn_plan()` (3902-4219); reopen validation 3936-3949; reopen
  execution ~4053; pre-claim+worktree 4080-4094; bg/fg split 4099-4108.
- Liveness: `_check_task_ownership()` `task_cmd.py:2634-2677`, invoked at 4078.
- Worktree create/reuse: `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py:1055-1121` (idempotent reuse 1071-1080).
- Sandbox: `internal/sandboxcmd/init.go` (`Provision`) + `bind.go`, via `worktree_cmd.py:1214-1256`.
- Handoff render: `render_handoff()` `task_cmd.py:3759-3821` (route to `respawn`, per E-1647).

## Behavior changes
### 1. Liveness guard → navigate, don't just error
Today `_check_task_ownership` raises when a different live session owns the task. For reopen,
branch on the live session's kind:
- **Foreground (tmux, has `process` pane):** `tmux switch-client -t <pane>` (**`switch-client`,
  not `select-window`** — works across sessions). Exit **without spawning**.
- **Background (`kind_id=2`):** print `attach with: endless task attach E-<id>` and exit.
- **No live session:** proceed. `--new-session` does NOT bypass a live owner.

### 2. Ensure / rebuild worktree + sandbox
- Present (not reaped): reuse (`worktree_cmd.py:1071-1080`).
- Reaped by land/drop: fresh branch **off `main`**. Collision policy: reuse `task/<id>-<slug>` if
  it still exists (drop case); a land-deleted branch means a fresh branch off main already holds
  the work. Decide + document the suffix rule.
- Sandbox: if reaped, re-`Provision` + re-`bind` (bind requires an existing sandbox — init first).

### 3. Inherit the most-applicable prior session (default)
Live-first is handled by the guard; among *ended* rows:
```sql
SELECT id, session_id, process, transcript_path, started_at, last_activity,
       (julianday(last_activity)-julianday(started_at))*86400 AS dur
FROM sessions WHERE active_task_id = :task AND state='ended'
ORDER BY (process IS NOT NULL OR transcript_path IS NOT NULL OR dur >= 10) DESC, started_at DESC
LIMIT 1;
```
- **Skip sub-10s ghosts; never order by `dur` desc** (Pattern B makes a stale row span days).
  Defends against E-1640 ghosts regardless of whether E-1640 has landed.
- Use the inherited session's worktree (maybe an alt) + its `outcome` + latest `session_statuses`
  to populate the E-1647 handoff vars. `--new-session` → fresh row + fresh worktree off main.

### 4. Render the flat respawn handoff
`render_handoff(..., task_type="respawn", restore_case=…, prior_outcome=…, last_status_snapshot=…)`.
Keep fg + `--bg` parity.

## Implementation note (makes verification possible)
Add a `--dry-run`/print seam to the reopen path that prints the **decision** (navigate-to-pane vs
spawn-fresh vs inherit-session=<id> vs new-session) and the resolved worktree path, WITHOUT
launching tmux/Claude. This makes the behaviors assertable in the verify script and is good
design regardless.

## Verification — create a per-task verify script
Create `tests/tasks/e-1645-verify.sh` in the `e-1624-verify.sh` shape (bash, `set -u`, the
`section`/`report_*`/`summary` helpers, `cd` repo root, ensure `go.work`, exit 0/1/2). Drive the
real reopen code path (via the `--dry-run` seam + sandbox fixtures); don't open real tmux.

The script asserts (named checks):
- **Resolver skips ghosts:** seed (fresh ids) one sub-10s ended ghost + one ≥10s ended real
  session on a task; the resolver returns the **real** session's id.
- **Reaped worktree → rebuild:** reopen of a task whose worktree was removed reports
  `restore_case=rebuilt-off-main`, a fresh branch off `main`, and a provisioned sandbox (assert
  via the dry-run decision + filesystem, not by spawning).
- **`--new-session`:** dry-run decision is `new-session` (fresh row + fresh worktree), not inherit.
- **Liveness guard:** with a live session bound to the task, the dry-run decision is
  `navigate` (`switch-client` for fg / `task attach` for bg), NOT spawn. (The actual
  `switch-client` side-effect is a manual check — note it in the script header.)

Re-runnability (sandbox NOT wiped between runs):
- Derive all seeded ids/`session_id`/`short_id` from freshly-allocated ids each run — never a
  fixed UNIQUE value.
- Capture seeding output; `exit 2` loudly on any setup/seed error (never /dev/null).
- Run the script at least TWICE before declaring it done.

**Verify-handoff — tell the user to verify with (only) this:**
```
esu && ./tests/tasks/e-1645-verify.sh
```

## Risks / open decisions
- Branch-name collision policy on reaped-but-branch-exists (drop case).
- `--bg` reopen has no interactive "ask the user the goal" step — confirm the respawn handoff
  still reads sensibly for a bg agent (it should ask in its first turn).

# E-1646 — `task respawn` alias + `session respawn <id> | --task E-NNN` entry points

**Epic:** E-1644 · **Blocked by:** E-1645 · **Layer:** Python CLI

Pure surface work on top of the E-1645 reopen core. The whole reason this exists is
**discoverability** — `respawn` was reached for and `--help` showed nothing.

## Files
- `src/endless/cli.py`: add `endless task respawn` near the spawn def (1559-1598); add
  `endless session respawn` to the session group (after ~line 926, before the `session status`
  subgroup at 928).
- `src/endless/task_cmd.py`: thin `task_respawn()` wrapper → `spawn_plan(..., reopen=True)`.
- `src/endless/session_cmd.py`: `session_respawn()` resolver + dispatch.

## Commands
### `endless task respawn <id>`
Exact alias for `endless task spawn <id> --reopen`. Pass through `--bg`, `--worktree`,
`--new-session`, `--force`. No new logic.

### `endless session respawn <session-id>`
Resolve the session row → its `active_task_id` + its **worktree path** (recorded cwd /
`worktree.json` / convention; may be an alt `e-<id>-<slug>`). Call the E-1645 reopen core
anchored via `--worktree <that path>`. Same liveness guard (navigate if a session is live).

### `endless session respawn --task E-NNN`
Resolve the **most-applicable** session via the E-1645 resolver (live-first; else newest ended
with `dur>=10s` OR populated `process`/`transcript_path`; **skip sub-10s ghosts**), then proceed
as `session respawn <id>`. `<session-id>` and `--task` are mutually exclusive; exactly one required.

## Notes
- Keep these wrappers dumb: all real behavior (guard, ensure-worktree, resolver, handoff) lives
  in E-1645's core so the two surfaces can't drift.

## Verification — create a per-task verify script
Create `tests/tasks/e-1646-verify.sh` in the `e-1624-verify.sh` shape (bash, `set -u`, the
`section`/`report_*`/`summary` helpers, `cd` repo root, ensure `go.work`, exit 0/1/2). Drive the
real CLI + the E-1645 dry-run seam against sandbox fixtures.

The script asserts (named checks):
- `endless task respawn --help` and `endless session respawn --help` exist (subcommands registered).
- `endless task --help` and `endless session --help` list `respawn`.
- `endless task respawn E-N` produces the **same** reopen decision as `endless task spawn E-N
  --reopen` (assert via the shared dry-run/print seam — identical resolved decision + worktree).
- `endless session respawn <id>` resolves to **that** session's worktree: seed an alt
  `e-<id>-<slug>` worktree for the session and assert the dry-run anchors there.
- `endless session respawn --task E-N` on a task with a seeded sub-10s ghost + a real session
  selects the **real** session.
- `--session-id` and `--task` together is rejected.

Re-runnability (sandbox NOT wiped between runs):
- Derive all seeded ids from freshly-allocated ids each run — never a fixed UNIQUE value.
- Capture seeding output; `exit 2` loudly on any setup/seed error (never /dev/null).
- Run the script at least TWICE before declaring it done.

**Verify-handoff — tell the user to verify with (only) this:**
```
esu && ./tests/tasks/e-1646-verify.sh
```

# E-1647 — Flat type-agnostic respawn handoff template

**Epic:** E-1644 · **Blocks:** E-1645 · **Layer:** Go template + Python render glue
